media.article:
A Beech Bonanza, N 3794N, crashed at night approximately 5 miles northwest of the Mason City Municipal Airport, Mason City, Iowa, at approximately 01:00, February 3, 1959. The pilot and three passengers were killed and the aircraft was demolished.
The aircraft was observed to take off toward the south in a normal manner, turn and climb to an estimated altitude of 800ft, and then head in a northwesterly direction. When approximately 5 miles had been traversed, the tail light of the aircraft was seen to descend gradually until it disappeared from sight. Follow this, many unsuccessful attempts were made to contact the aircraft by radio. The wreckage was found in a field later that morning.
This accident, like so many before it, was caused by the pilot’s decision to undertake a flight in which the likelihood of encountering instrument conditions existed, in the mistaken belief that he could cope with en route instrument weather conditions, without having the necessary familiarization with the instruments in the aircraft and without being properly certificated to fly solely by instrument.

subs.stem: What happened to the aircraft and the people on board?
options: A.  The aircraft was slightly damaged while the pilot died.
B.  The aircraft was totally destroyed while the pilot survived.
C.  The aircraft was totally destroyed while the pilot was killed.
D.  The aircraft was severely damaged and the pilot was injured.
answer: C.  The aircraft was totally destroyed while the pilot was killed.

translation.article:
一架比奇“幸运”飞机 N3794N，于 1959 年 2 月 3 日凌晨约 01:00，在爱荷华州梅森市梅森市立机场西北约 5 英里处坠毁。飞行员和三名乘客遇难，飞机损毁。
有人观察到该机以正常方式向南起飞，转弯爬升至估计高度 800 英尺，然后向西北方向飞行。当飞行约 5 英里后，该机的尾灯被看到逐渐下降直至消失。随后，多次尝试通过无线电联系该机均未成功。当天晚些时候，残骸在一块田地里被发现。
这起事故与之前的许多事故一样，是由飞行员决定在可能存在仪表飞行条件的情况下执行飞行所导致的，他错误地认为自己能够应对航路上的仪表气象条件，而实际上他对飞机上的仪表缺乏必要的熟悉，并且没有经过适当的认证来仅凭仪表飞行。

media.article:
Lack of familiarity with the English language and poor radio transmission discipline is a problem for foreign pilots，“Quite often they gossip in Mandarin on a main control frequency about the price of vegetables”, said one pilot. There are still many areas in China where position reports are made on HF radio and despite China’s prowess in launching satellites for foreign customers, satellite communications are still a long way from providing a viable alternative.
Pilots also find the controllers use draconian power, possibly as a result of military influence. Controllers have closed an entire airway due the weather. They have been obliged to do this because of the military’s refusal to allow deviation from the airway. Similarly traffic has been obliged to divert from airport because the controllers considered the weather to be unacceptable. This can be embarrassing for pilots who have flight planned fuel reserves with the actual and forecast weather. Fortunately, as a result of pressure from the airlines, the Chinese are starting to yield to the standard practice of allowing the aircraft commander to judge conditions himself before making an approach.
China has been cautious in venturing into the field of satellite-based navigation systems. Forty eight of China’s airport are now equipped with Instrument Landing Systems (ILS). Beijing and Shanghai Hongqiao are the only airports with Category II ILS approaches, and with the addition of Guangzhou airport in the south, are the only international gateway airports in China.
Pilots operating regularly in China are optimistic about improvement. General operations manager of Dragon Air Felix Hart says that in the last three years he has noticed enormous improvements, especially in the level of English language comprehension. Another pilot said: “Although they are still reluctant to give radar vectors, they will often tell us how far we are from the ILS centerline and we know that they have us on radar when they ask us to squawk our SSR transponder identity”. He cautions:“Many controllers lack terrain awareness, so we have to be very careful about following their instructions”.

answer: A.  they have little transmission discipline

translation.stem: 以下哪项不正确？
options: A. 大多数管制员英语较差
B. 中国总共有 3 个国际门户机场
C. 许多管制员缺乏地形意识
D. 卫星通信将很快投入使用

translation.article:
对英语语言不熟悉以及糟糕的无线电通话纪律是外国飞行员面临的一个问题。“他们经常在一个主用管制频率上用普通话闲聊蔬菜的价格，”一位飞行员说道。在中国，许多地区仍然使用高频无线电进行位置报告，尽管中国在为外国客户发射卫星方面实力雄厚，但卫星通信距离提供可行的替代方案仍有很长的路要走。
飞行员还发现管制员使用过于严苛的权力，这可能是受军方影响的结果。管制员曾因天气原因关闭整条航路。他们被迫这样做是因为军方不允许偏离航路。同样，交通流量也曾因管制员认为天气不可接受而被迫从机场改航。这对于那些根据实际和预报天气计划了燃油储备的飞行员来说可能会很尴尬。幸运的是，由于来自航空公司的压力，中国方面开始逐渐接受标准做法，即允许机长在执行进近前自行判断条件。
中国在涉足基于卫星的导航系统领域一直持谨慎态度。中国目前有 48 个机场配备了仪表着陆系统。北京和上海虹桥是仅有的具备 II 类仪表着陆系统进近的机场，加上南方的广州机场，是中国仅有的国际门户机场。
定期在中国运行的飞行员对改进持乐观态度。港龙航空的总经理 Felix Hart 表示，在过去三年中，他注意到巨大的改进，尤其是在英语理解水平方面。另一位飞行员说：“尽管他们仍然不愿意提供雷达引导，但他们经常告诉我们距离 ILS 中心线有多远，而且我们知道当要求我们应答 SSR 应答机识别码时，他们已经在雷达上看到了我们。”他提醒道：“许多管制员缺乏地形意识，所以我们必须非常小心地遵循他们的指令。”

media.article:
Automation promises great results for the controller. Technical advances are expected to improve the performance of the human-machine system, and controllers assumed to be flexible enough to adapt to the new working environment. But partial automation can actually increase the risk of error attributable to human operators. This is because they are expected to monitor systems that are harder to understand and to exercise their skills only intermittently.
In the US, the FAA’s Automated En-route ATC programme is designed to detect potential conflicts up to 20 minutes before they occur. It generates a resolution immediately, and provides simulation capabilities.
As a result, the controller deals only with aircraft that the system says need attention, and while he/she has to rely on the system to detect problems and provide resolutions. The controller resolves them as soon as they are detected rather than wait to assess them visually on the situation display, so the focus of attention shifts from tactical monitoring, problem analysis and coordination toward strategic planning tasks.
Many French controllers query this philosophy. Applied to French airspace, it could, mean a controller has to transmit a revised clearance to an aircraft in his sector to avoid a predicted conflict in another sector with an aircraft currently in a third. And if he disagrees with the clearance, he will have to propose an alternative without having access to information on what is happening in the other two sectors. If the controller’s role is limited to transmitting machine-generated clearances, he will soon lose his ability to make control decisions.
CENA is developing an alternative at its Toulouse training centre. The En-Route Air Traffic Organizer (ERATO) is a software program to cope with what CENA terms the capacity barrier. It takes all the information available from sensors and flight plans, plus intention data obtained by data link from aircraft to create a working airspace model. The scenario it presents can identify and prioritize potential conflicts, and offer tool to resolve them.
To do this, ERATO enlists the support of artificial intelligence techniques. It can describe the mental activity of controller, and assess deterioration under pressure of time, fatigue and stress. The central question is to identify the conditions under which the controller’s knowledge ceases to be accessible. It also sets out to identify and predict sources of error.

translation.stem: ______旨在识别管制员知识变得不可访问的条件。
options: A. 航路空中交通组织器
D. FAA 的自动化航路 ATC 计划

translation.article:
自动化为管制员带来了巨大的前景。技术进步预计将提高人机系统的性能，并且管制员被认为具有足够的灵活性来适应新的工作环境。但部分自动化实际上可能会增加人为操作失误的风险。这是因为他们被期望监控更难理解的系统，并且只能间歇性地运用他们的技能。
在美国，FAA 的自动化航路 ATC 计划旨在检测潜在冲突，最早可在冲突发生前 20 分钟发现。它会立即生成解决方案，并提供模拟能力。
因此，管制员只处理系统提示需要注意的飞机，并且他/她必须依赖系统来检测问题并提供解决方案。管制员一旦检测到问题就立即解决，而不是等待在态势显示屏上目视评估，因此关注的焦点从战术监控、问题分析和协调转向战略规划任务。
许多法国管制员质疑这种理念。应用于法国空域，这可能意味着管制员必须向其扇区内的某一架飞机发送修改后的许可，以避免与当前在第三个扇区的另一架飞机在另一个扇区内发生预测冲突。如果他不认同该许可，他将不得不在无法获取其他两个扇区正在发生的信息的情况下提出替代方案。如果管制员的角色仅限于传输机器生成的许可，他将很快丧失做出管制决策的能力。
CENA 正在其图卢兹培训中心开发一种替代方案。航路空中交通组织器是一个软件程序，旨在应对 CENA 所谓的容量瓶颈。它获取来自传感器和飞行计划的所有可用信息，以及通过数据链从飞机获得的意图数据，以创建一个工作空域模型。它呈现的场景可以识别和优先处理潜在冲突，并提供解决工具。
为此，ERATO 采用了人工智能技术。它可以描述管制员的思维活动，并评估在时间压力、疲劳和压力下的能力衰减。核心问题是识别管制员知识变得不可访问的条件。它还着手识别和预测错误源。

media.article: We noticed a flash of lightning that just occurred at the north of our track. Using the WX radar we were only able to paint light to medium precipitation at north of our track. Looking ahead visually we saw nothing but clear sky and stars above, but there was no moon yet and it was very dark. The dispatcher said in an ASARS (aircraft communication addressing and reporting system) message just minutes before that all the rides ahead had been reported smooth. All of a sudden the overspeed EICAS and aural warnings went off and the captain immediately retarded the throttles and turned off the auto throttles and autopilot. He told me to turn on the seatbelt sign and the ignition switches to continuous. He was battling very hard as we immediately started climbing at about 3000 fpm. I then told the flight attendants over the PA to take their seats immediately. The captain finally got control of the aircraft as we were approaching FL370, and then eased it back down to FL350. The turbulence we encountered was moderate to severe as for a few moments we were unable to control the aircraft. During the steep climb I made a transmission to any other aircraft that might be along our track, but fortunately we were ahead of the track that night. We talked to the flight attendants right away and it has aggravated a previous back injury. No passenger was even out of their seats so that was good. No other flights behind us encountered anything other than a little light to moderate chop. We notified commercial radio immediately and had them pass everything along to dispatch, who talked with us eventually on a phone patch. We made a logbook entry and after a cabin inspection and group discussion we deemed the aircraft able to continue on to our destination.

translation.article: 我们注意到航迹北侧刚刚发生了一道闪电。使用气象雷达，我们只能探测到航迹北侧有轻到中度的降水。向前目视观察，除了上方的晴朗天空和星星外，什么也看不到，但没有月亮，天色非常暗。就在几分钟前，签派员在 ASARS 信息中说，前方所有航段的 ride 报告均为平稳。突然，超速 EICAS 和语音警告响起，机长立即收回油门，关断自动油门和自动驾驶。他让我打开安全带指示灯并将点火开关置于连续位。他非常艰难地操控着，因为我们立即以约 3000 英尺/分钟的速率开始爬升。然后我通过客舱广播通知乘务员立即就座。当我们接近 FL370 时，机长终于控制住了飞机，然后将其缓缓降回 FL350。我们遭遇的颠簸从中度到严重，有几分钟我们无法控制飞机。在剧烈爬升过程中，我向可能沿我们航迹飞行的其他飞机进行了通播，但幸运的是那晚我们在航迹前方。我们立即与乘务员进行了沟通，这加重了之前的一次背部受伤。没有乘客离开座位，所以还不错。我们后面的其他航班除了遇到一些轻到中度颠簸外，没有遇到其他情况。我们立即通知了商用无线电，让他们将所有信息传递给签派，后者最终通过电话 patch 与我们取得了联系。我们填写了飞行记录本，在进行了客舱检查和小组讨论后，我们认为飞机可以继续飞往目的地。

media.article:
A seaplane is defined as “an airplane designed to take off from and land on water”. Seaplanes can be generally classified as either flying boats, or floatplanes. Those that can be operated on both land and water are called amphibians.
The floatplane is ordinarily understood to be a conventional landplane equipped with separate floats instead of wheels, as opposed to a flying boat in which the hull serves the dual purpose of providing buoyancy in the water and space for the pilot, crew, and passengers. The float type is the more common seaplane, particularly those with relatively low horsepower. It may be equipped with either singly float or twin floats; however, most seaplanes are the twinboat variety. Though there is considerable difference between handling a floatplane and handling a flying boat, the theory on which the techniques are based is similar. Therefore, with few exceptions, the explanations given here for one type may be considered to apply to the other. In the air the seaplane is operated and controlled in much the same manner as the landplane, since the only major difference between the floatplane and the landplane is the installation of floats instead of wheels. Generally, because of the float’s greater weight, replacing wheels with floats increases the airplane’s empty weight and thus decreases its useful load, and rate of climb.

translation.stem: 以下哪项陈述是正确的？
options: A. 浮筒式水上飞机和船身式水上飞机的操作差异很大，因为技术基于不同的理论
B. 就空重而言，水上飞机比陆上飞机携带更多的有效载荷
C. 就空重而言，陆上飞机的爬升率高于水上飞机
D. 水上飞机比陆上飞机更常用，因为它们可以在水上着陆

translation.article:
水上飞机被定义为“一种设计用于从水面起飞和在水面上降落的飞机”。水上飞机通常可分为船身式水上飞机或浮筒式水上飞机。那些既能在陆地上又能在水面上运行的被称为两栖飞机。
浮筒式水上飞机通常被理解为是一种传统的陆上飞机，配备了单独的浮筒代替机轮，而船身式水上飞机的机身则兼具提供水面浮力和容纳飞行员、机组人员及乘客的空间双重用途。浮筒式是更常见的水上飞机类型，尤其是那些马力相对较低的。它可以配备单浮筒或双浮筒；然而，大多数水上飞机是双浮筒类型。尽管操作浮筒式水上飞机和船身式水上飞机之间存在相当大的差异，但这些技术所基于的理论是相似的。因此，除了少数例外，此处对一种类型的解释可视为适用于另一种类型。在空中，水上飞机的操作和控制方式与陆上飞机大致相同，因为浮筒式水上飞机与陆上飞机之间的唯一主要区别在于安装了浮筒而不是机轮。通常，由于浮筒重量较大，用浮筒替换机轮会增加飞机的空重，从而减少其有效载荷和爬升率。

media.article:
The FAA established categories based upon aircraft approach speeds to make primary minima as safe as possible. Here are the actual definitions according to the pilot/controller glossary:
Aircraft Approach Category——A grouping of aircraft based on a speed of 1.3 times the stall speed in the landing configuration at maximum gross landing weight. An aircraft shall be in only one category. If it is necessary to maneuver at speeds in excess of the upper limit of a speed range for a category, the minimum for the next higher category should be used. For example, an aircraft is in Category A, but is circling to land at a speed in excess of 91 knots, should use the approach Category B minimums when circling to land. The categories are as follows:
(1) Category A - speed less than 91 knots.
(2) Category B - speed 91 knots or more, but less than 121 knots.
(3) Category C - speed 121 knots or more, but less than 141 knots.
(4) Category D - speed 141 knots or more but less than 166 knots.
(5) Category E - speed 166 knots or more.
You’ll notice the visibility requirements increase as the category speeds increase. That’s because the turn radius is greater at the higher speeds and the aircraft will need more room and consequently more visibility to maneuver in.
Two descent minimums, the DH and the MDA, should be defined:
Decision Height - With respect to the operation of aircraft, means the height at which a decision must be made during an ILS, MLS, or PAR instrument approach to either continue the approach or to execute a missed approach.
Minimum Descent Altitude/MDA - The lowest altitude, expressed in feet above mean sea level, to which descent is authorized on final approach or during circle-to-land maneuvering in execution of a standard instrument approach procedure where no electronic glide slope is provided.
The DH is a go-no-go altitude, and you have absolutely no choice in the matter. If you can see enough of the runway environment upon reaching the DH to continue and land safely, you do so. If you don’t see the runway environment you go around immediately.
The MDA affords a little more time to sort things out. With no electronic glide slope, you will reach the MDA at an uncertain distance from the runway. Once at the MDA, you may fly out the time to the MAP (missed approach point). You cannot descend below the MDA until the aircraft is in a position to make a normal approach to the runway.

subs.stem: Which of the following statements is not true?

B.  A go-no-go decision must be made on reaching MDA

D.  A go-no-go decision must be based on DH or MDA

translation.article:
FAA 根据飞机进近速度建立了分类，以使最低标准尽可能安全。以下是飞行员/管制员术语表中的实际定义：
飞机进近类别——基于飞机在最大着陆重量下着陆构型中 1.3 倍失速速度的飞机分组。一架飞机只能属于一个类别。如果必须以超过某一类别速度范围上限的速度进行机动飞行，则应使用下一更高类别的最低标准。例如，一架飞机属于 A 类，但以超过 91 节的速度进行盘旋着陆，则在盘旋着陆时应使用 B 类进近最低标准。类别如下：
(1) A 类 - 速度低于 91 节。
(2) B 类 - 速度 91 节或以上，但低于 121 节。
(3) C 类 - 速度 121 节或以上，但低于 141 节。
(4) D 类 - 速度 141 节或以上但低于 166 节。
(5) E 类 - 速度 166 节或以上。
你会注意到，能见度要求随类别速度的增加而增加。这是因为在更高速度下转弯半径更大，飞机将需要更多空间，从而需要更高的能见度来进行机动飞行。
应定义两种下降最低标准：决断高和最低下降高度：
决断高 - 就飞机运行而言，指在 ILS、MLS 或 PAR 仪表进近过程中必须做出继续进近或执行复飞决断的高度。
最低下降高度 - 在未提供电子下滑道的标准仪表进近程序的最后进近或盘旋着陆机动飞行中，授权下降的最低高度，以平均海平面以上英尺数表示。
DH 是“能进则进，不能则复飞”的高度，在此问题上你完全没有选择。如果在到达 DH 时你能看到足够的跑道环境以继续安全着陆，你就着陆。如果看不到跑道环境，你立即复飞。
MDA 提供了多一点时间来做出判断。在没有电子下滑道的情况下，你将在距离跑道不确定的距离处到达 MDA。一旦到达 MDA，你可以飞到 MAP 的时间点。在飞机处于能够进行正常进近着陆的位置之前，你不能下降到 MDA 以下。

media.article:
Besides seeking information on the weather, the pilot also relies on the very important information contained within aeronautical publications. These tell the pilot of the availability of radar, radio frequencies, landing and weather minima, glide slope angles, and the types of approach available at a particular runway. The terminal or approach plates contain further information. They give a runway and airfield pictorial presentation of the obstructions surrounding the field, the approach lighting, and the relative position of the NAVAIDs. Additionally, they give the length and width of the runways, as well as information on rates of descent and ground speeds. All of this may be needed by the pilot in order to execute the approach.
As previously mentioned, there are a variety of approaches used around the world at different airports. These approaches are divided into two major categories: precision and non-precision. Non-precision approach procedures are intended to provide an orderly and safe flight progression from the en-route structure to a position at an aerodrome from which a visual landing can be made. VOR, VORTAC, NDB and airport surveillance radar (ASR) are all examples of non-precision approaches. The only precision approaches available anywhere are the precision radar approach (PAR), the instrument landing system (ILS) and the new microwave landing system. Of these three, the ILS is the most prevalently used.
On a precision radar approach，the controller interprets radar displays and transmits course and glide slope information to the pilot. The pilot then maneuvers the aircraft as directed by the controller in order to arrive at a position from which to land safely.
The instrument landing system (ILS) is designed to provide an approach path for exact alignment and descent of an aircraft on its final approach to a runway. An ILS consists of a highly directional localizer and glide slope transmitter with associated marker beacons and at some sites，distance measuring equipment (DME). There are three ILS categories: category I equipment gives guidance information down to a decision height (DH) of not less than 200ft, while category Ⅱ gives a DH of not less than 100ft, and category Ⅲ ILS approach are subdivided into three groups, all of which allow descents to touchdown, since decision height are not specified.

translation.article:
除了获取天气信息外，飞行员还依赖于航空出版物中包含的非常重要的信息。这些信息告知飞行员雷达可用性、无线电频率、着陆和天气最低标准、下滑道角度以及特定跑道可用的进近类型。终端图或进近图包含更多信息。它们提供跑道和机场的障碍物、进近灯光以及导航台相对位置的图示。此外，它们还提供跑道的长度和宽度，以及下降率和地速的信息。飞行员可能需要所有这些信息来执行进近。
如前所述，世界各地不同机场使用多种进近方式。这些进近分为两大类：精密进近和非精密进近。非精密进近程序旨在提供从航路结构到机场某位置的井然有序且安全的飞行进程，以便进行目视着陆。VOR、VORTAC、NDB 和机场监视雷达都是非精密进近的例子。唯一可用的精密进近是精密雷达进近、仪表着陆系统和新的微波着陆系统。在这三者中，ILS 使用最为普遍。
在精密雷达进近中，管制员解读雷达显示并向飞行员传输航向和下滑道信息。然后飞行员按照管制员的指令操纵飞机，以到达能够安全着陆的位置。
仪表着陆系统旨在为飞机在最后进近至跑道时提供精确对准和下降的进近路径。ILS 由一个高方向性的航向信标和下滑道信标以及相关的指点标组成，在某些地点还配有测距设备。有三种 ILS 类别：I 类设备提供低至不低于 200 英尺决断高的引导信息，II 类提供不低于 100 英尺的决断高，III 类 ILS 进近又分为三个子组，所有这些都允许下降至接地，因为未规定决断高。

media.article:
SIDs, STARs, and other abbreviated clearances have been developed because of the need to put as many aircraft as possible into and through the ATC system. To accomplish this, it becomes necessary to keep the radio transmissions as brief as possible; hence, the use of abbreviations like “as filed”, and the use of SIDs and STARs.
Another reason for these SIDs, and such, is to put them down on paper in such a manner as to simplify what otherwise could be a difficult clearance to copy, read back, understand, and comply with. In order for this to work, however, pilots have the responsibility to thoroughly understand the restrictions involved.
The Jeppesen Airway Manual introduction has this to say about SIDs: Pilots of IFR civil aircraft operation from locations where SID procedures are effective may expect ATC clearances containing a SID. Use of a SID requires possession of at least the textual description of the SID. Pilots not desiring to use the SID are expected to advise ATC. Notification may be accomplished by filing “NO SID” in the remarks section of the filed flight plan or by the less desirable method of verbally advising ATC.
I can’t see any reason to turn down a SID as long as you have the required textual portion in your possession and your aircraft can meet the various restrictions, which are explained later. Turning it down will only make more work for everyone involved. If you filed “NO SID”, the only difference is that you will get the complete textual portion read to you, and you’ll have to copy it down and read it back, which is sometimes very complicated and time-consuming. After all, ATC has devised the departure routings to expedite traffic and to give adequate separation between inbound and outbound flights, so it stands to reason that as long as you will be departing on that route anyway, you might as well make it easy on all concerned by having the SIDs in your possession and reviewing them before flight.

translation.article:
SID、STAR 和其他简化许可的产生，是因为需要将尽可能多的飞机纳入 ATC 系统并使其通过该系统。为了实现这一目标，必须使无线电通话尽可能简短；因此，使用了诸如“按提交计划”之类的缩写，以及使用了 SID 和 STAR。
制定这些 SID 等的另一个原因是将它们以书面形式呈现，以简化原本可能难以抄收、复诵、理解和遵守的许可。然而，为了使这一点奏效，飞行员有责任彻底理解所涉及的限制。
Jeppesen 航路手册引言中关于 SID 的说明如下：从适用 SID 程序的机场起飞的 IFR 民用航空器飞行员，可能会收到包含 SID 的 ATC 许可。使用 SID 需要至少持有 SID 的文字描述。不希望使用 SID 的飞行员应通知 ATC。可通过在提交的飞行计划备注栏中填写“NO SID”来通知，或者通过不太可取的口头通知 ATC 的方式。
只要你持有所需的文字部分并且你的飞机能够满足各种限制（稍后将解释），我想不出任何理由拒绝 SID。拒绝只会给所有相关人员增加更多工作量。如果你提交了“NO SID”，唯一的区别是你将听到完整的文字描述被读给你，你必须抄收下来并复诵，这有时非常复杂且耗时。毕竟，ATC 设计离场航线是为了加快交通流量并给进港和离港航班之间提供足够的间隔，所以道理很简单：只要你将在那条航线上离港，你最好持有 SID 并在飞行前复习它们，以便让所有相关人员都更轻松。

media.article:
One day in March 1944, I was flying my Spitfire from Italy toward our base on Corsica. I was alone and it was late in the day. The sky was overcast and gray, as was the sea surface below, so my horizon was marginal. But I had no worries about my situation, and was not paying attention to the instruments. Suddenly, a change of air and engine noise told me something was wrong. I looked at my instruments and they showed my plane was in a diving turn. My reaction was, “those instrument can’t be right!”, but I knew immediately that they were right and that I had to rely on them to get out of the dive. I was at about 6000ft and had sufficient altitude to recover using the needle-ball-airspeed instrument and link-trainer time. For about a year and a half after graduation, I got much instrument and link-trainer time. I became fully competent in the needle-ball-airspeed technique, so I never used the artificial horizon, because its gyro would tumble if you made a steep turn. Incidentally, the Spitfire, instead of a ball to show skidding or slipping, had a second needle, pointing downward below the turn needle.
I probably lost my horizon after flying into haze, or a region of uniform color and brightness. If I had flown into cloud I would have known that I had lost my horizon and had to go on instruments in order to stay oriented and in control. If I had been unable to fly on instruments I would have become disoriented, a condition called pilot vertigo, and I would have had to bail out or crash with the plane. But I didn’t suffer vertigo because I was not aware that I had lost my horizon, orientation, and control of the plane. It then wandered and went into a spiral dive producing the noises that alerted me.
I recalled this experience in July 1999 when John F.Kennndy, Jr’s plane crashed. It was very clear to me, from the radar records of his plane’s behavior that the same thing happened to him.

translation.article:
1944 年 3 月的一天，我驾驶我的喷火式战斗机从意大利飞往我们在科西嘉岛的基地。我独自一人，天色已晚。天空阴云密布，灰蒙蒙的，下方的海面也是如此，所以我的天地线很模糊。但我对自己的处境并不担心，也没有注意仪表。突然，发动机声音和气流的改变告诉我出问题了。我看了看仪表，它们显示我的飞机正在转弯俯冲。我的第一反应是“这些仪表不可能对！”，但我立刻意识到它们是对的，我必须依靠它们来改出俯冲。我当时在大约 6000 英尺的高度，有足够的高度使用针球空速仪技术和在 link 训练器上积累的经验来改出。毕业后的大约一年半时间里，我进行了大量的仪表和 link 训练器训练。我完全掌握了针球空速仪技术，所以我从不使用人工地平仪，因为如果你进行大坡度转弯，它的陀螺就会倾倒。顺便说一句，喷火式战斗机不是用小球来表示侧滑，而是有一个在转弯指针下方指向下方的第二根指针。
我可能是在飞入霾层或颜色和亮度均匀的区域后失去了天地线。如果我飞入云中，我会知道自己失去了天地线，必须依靠仪表来保持方向和姿态。如果我无法依靠仪表飞行，我就会迷失方向，这种情况称为飞行员眩晕，我将不得不跳伞或随机坠毁。但我没有遭受眩晕，因为我并没有意识到自己已经失去了天地线、方向感和对飞机的控制。飞机随后飘摆并进入螺旋俯冲，产生的噪音提醒了我。
1999 年 7 月小约翰·F·肯尼迪的飞机坠毁时，我想起了这段经历。从雷达记录他飞机的行为来看，我很清楚同样的事情发生在他身上。

media.article:
The effect of pressure altitude and ambient temperature is to define primarily the density altitude and its effect on takeoff performance. While subsequent corrections are appropriate for the effect of temperature on certain items of power plant performance, density altitude defines specific effects on takeoff performance. An increase in density altitude can produce a two-fold effect on takeoff performance: (l) greater takeoff speed and (2) decreased thrust and reduced net accelerating force. If an airplane of given weight and configuration is operated at greater heights above standard sea level, the airplane will still require the same dynamic pressure to become airborne at the takeoff lift coefficient. Thus, the airplane at altitude will take off at the same indicated airspeed as at sea level, but because of the reduced air density, the true airspeed will be greater.
The effect of density altitude on power plant thrust depends much on the type of power plant. An increase in altitude above standard sea level will bring an immediate decrease in power output for the unsupercharged reciprocating engine. However, an increase in altitude above standard sea level will not cause a decrease in power output for the supercharged reciprocating engine until the altitude exceeds the critical operating altitude. For those power plants which experience a decay in thrust with an increase in altitude, the effect on the net accelerating force and acceleration rate can be approximated by assuming a direct variation with density. Actually, this assumed variation would closely approximate the effect on airplanes with high thrust-to-weight ratios.
Proper accounting of pressure altitude and temperature is mandatory for accurate prediction of takeoff roll distance.

subs.stem: An increase in density altitude can lead to ______ ．

translation.stem: 密度高度的增加可能导致______。

subs.stem: An airplane operating at altitude requires ______ at sea level.
options: A.  greater dynamic pressure for takeoff as
B.  greater true airspeed for takeoff as
C.  greater indicated airspeed for takeoff as
answer: B.  greater true airspeed for takeoff as

subs.stem: Which of the following statements is not true?
options: A.  Density altitude is influenced by pressure altitude and ambient temperature.
B.  The thrust variation caused by density altitude depends much on the type of power plants.
C.  To make accurate prediction of takeoff distance, we must consider pressure altitude and temperature
D.  The power output for the supercharged reciprocating engine does not change with altitude.
answer: D.  The power output for the supercharged reciprocating engine does not change with altitude.

translation.stem: 以下哪项陈述不正确？
options: A. 密度高度受气压高度和环境温度影响。
B. 密度高度引起的推力变化在很大程度上取决于动力装置的类型。
C. 为了准确预测起飞距离，我们必须考虑气压高度和温度。
D. 增压活塞式发动机的功率输出不随高度变化。

translation.article:
气压高度和环境温度的影响主要在于定义密度高度及其对起飞性能的影响。虽然后续修正适用于温度对动力装置某些性能参数的影响，但密度高度定义了对起飞性能的特定影响。密度高度的增加会对起飞性能产生双重影响：(1) 更大的起飞速度和 (2) 推力下降以及净加速力减小。如果一架给定重量和构型的飞机在高于标准海平面的高度运行，该飞机仍需要相同的动压才能在起飞升力系数下离地。因此，飞机在高海拔起飞时的指示空速与在海平面相同，但由于空气密度降低，真空速会更大。
密度高度对动力装置推力的影响在很大程度上取决于动力装置的类型。对于非增压活塞式发动机，高度高于标准海平面将导致功率输出立即下降。然而，对于增压活塞式发动机，在高度超过临界工作高度之前，高于标准海平面的高度增加不会导致功率输出下降。对于那些推力随高度增加而衰减的动力装置，对净加速力和加速率的影响可以通过假设与密度成正比的变化来近似。实际上，这种假设的变化可以很好地近似于具有高推重比的飞机的影响。
正确核算气压高度和温度对于准确预测起飞滑跑距离是强制性的。

media.article:
In addition to the important factors of proper technique, many other variables affect the landing performance of an airplane. Any item which alters the landing speed or deceleration rate during the landing roll will affect the landing distance.
The effect of gross weight on landing distance is one of the principal items determining the landing distance of an airplane. One effect of an increased gross weight is that the airplane will require a greater speed to support the airplane at the landing angle of attack and lift coefficient.
The minimum landing distance will vary in direct proportion to the gross weight. For example, a 10 percent increase in gross weight at landing would cause:
(1) a 5 percent increase in landing velocity.
(2) a 10 percent increase in landing distance.
The effect of wind on landing distance is large and deserves proper consideration when predicting landing distance. Since the airplane will land at a particular airspeed independent of the wind, the principal effect of wind on landing distance is due to the change in the ground speed at which the airplane touches down. The effect of wind on deceleration during the landing is identical to the effect on acceleration during the takeoff.
A headwind which is 10 percent of the landing airspeed will reduce the landing distance approximately 19 percent but a tailwind which is 10 percent of the landing speed will increase the landing distance approximately 21 percent.
The effect of pressure altitude and ambient temperature is to define density altitude and its effect on landing performance. An increase in density altitude will increase landing speed but will not alter the net retarding force. Since an increase in altitude does not alter deceleration, the effect of density altitude on landing distance would actually be due to the greater TAS (true airspeed).
The minimum landing distance at 5000ft would be 16 percent greater than the minimum landing distance at sea level. The approximate increase in landing distance with altitude is approximately 3 and a half percent for each 1000ft of altitude. Proper accounting of density altitude is necessary to accurately predict landing distance.
The effect of proper landing speed is important when runway lengths and landing distances are critical. The landing speeds specified in the airplane’s flight handbooks is generally the minimum safe speeds at which the airplane can be landed. Any attempt to land at below the specified speed may mean that the airplane may stall, be difficult to control, or develop high rates of descent. On the other hand, an excessive speed at landing may improve the controllability slightly, but will cause an undesirable increase in landing distance.

subs.stem: A 40 percent increase in gross weight at landing would result in ______ ．
options: A.  a 40 percent increase in landing roll
B.  a 20 percent decrease in landing velocity
C.  a 20 percent increase in landing distance
D.  a 10 percent increase in landing speed
answer: A.  a 40 percent increase in landing roll

subs.stem: Landing at below minimum safe speeds may result in ______ ．

translation.stem: 低于最低安全速度着陆可能导致______。

translation.article:
除了正确技术这一重要因素外，许多其他变量也会影响飞机的着陆性能。任何改变着陆速度或着陆滑跑减速率的因素都会影响着陆距离。
全重对着陆距离的影响是决定飞机着陆距离的主要因素之一。全重增加的一个影响是飞机需要更大的速度来在着陆迎角和升力系数下支撑飞机。
最小着陆距离将随全重成比例变化。例如，着陆时全重增加 10% 将导致：
(1) 着陆速度增加 5%。
(2) 着陆距离增加 10%。
风对着陆距离的影响很大，在预测着陆距离时应予以适当考虑。由于飞机将以特定的空速着陆（与风无关），风对着陆距离的主要影响是由于飞机接地时地速的变化。风对着陆减速的影响与对起飞加速的影响相同。
相当于着陆空速 10% 的顶风将使着陆距离减少约 19%，但相当于着陆速度 10% 的顺风将使着陆距离增加约 21%。
气压高度和环境温度的影响在于定义密度高度及其对着陆性能的影响。密度高度的增加将增加着陆速度，但不会改变净减速力。由于高度的增加不会改变减速率，密度高度对着陆距离的影响实际上是由于更大的真空速。
在 5000 英尺高度的最小着陆距离将比海平面的最小着陆距离大 16%。着陆距离随高度增加的近似值约为每 1000 英尺高度增加 3.5%。正确核算密度高度对于准确预测着陆距离是必要的。
当跑道长度和着陆距离很关键时，正确着陆速度的影响很重要。飞机飞行手册中规定的着陆速度通常是飞机可以安全着陆的最低速度。任何试图以低于规定速度着陆的尝试都可能意味着飞机可能失速、难以操控或产生高下降率。另一方面，着陆时速度过大可能会稍微改善可操控性，但会导致着陆距离不希望的增加。

media.article:
We have mentioned that at any point in the atmosphere, pressure is the weight of the air above that point. Since pressure is the weight of the air above, and since less and less air lies above a point as it moves upward through the atmosphere, pressure must decrease with increasing altitude. The greater pressure at low altitude compresses the air more than does the lesser pressure at higher altitude. Therefore, the rate of decrease (lapse rate) in pressure with height becomes less with increasing altitude. For example, from sea level to 1000ft, pressure drops about one inch of mercury; but from 19000 to 20000ft, pressure drops only about six-tenths of an inch. The rate of decrease of pressure with height, however, is not always constant. Like most substances, air contracts as it cools and expands as it becomes warmer. Therefore, when a sample of air cools, it occupies less space; when heated, it occupies more. As a result, the rate of pressure decrease with height in cold air is greater than in warm air.
Since air is a gas, it may be compressed or permitted to expand. when air is compressed, a given volume contains more air, hence its density, or weight, is increased. Conversely, when air is permitted to expand, a given volume contains less air, thus its density, or weight, is decreased. Heat is a property of all matter. From early studies of science, we learned that heat is the motion of molecules. Heat is then defined as the total energy of motion of molecules. We also learned that dense air has more molecules than less dense air. The two might have the same average motion, and thus have the same temperature, but the total energy, and consequently the degree of heat is greater in the dense air with more molecules. We cannot measure heat directly, but we can measure temperature with the thermometer.

translation.stem: 一点上方的压力随高度降低而______。

translation.stem: 暖空气中压力的递减率______冷空气中的递减率。

translation.stem: 当空气被压缩时，给定体积比未压缩时含有______空气。

subs.stem: If the temperature is the same, the total energy of dense air is ______ that of less dense air.

translation.stem: 如果温度相同，稠密空气的总能量______较稀薄空气的总能量。

translation.article:
我们已经提到，在大气中的任何一点，压力都是该点上方空气的重量。由于压力是上方空气的重量，并且随着一点在大气中向上移动，其上方的空气越来越少，因此压力必须随高度的增加而减小。低海拔处较大的压力对空气的压缩程度高于高海拔处较小的压力。因此，压力随高度递减的速率随着高度的增加而减小。例如，从海平面到 1000 英尺，压力下降约一英寸汞柱；但从 19000 英尺到 20000 英尺，压力仅下降约十分之六英寸。然而，压力随高度递减的速率并不总是恒定的。与大多数物质一样，空气冷却时收缩，变暖时膨胀。因此，当一团空气冷却时，它占据的空间更小；加热时，占据的空间更大。结果是，冷空气中压力随高度递减的速率大于暖空气中的速率。
由于空气是一种气体，它可以被压缩或允许膨胀。当空气被压缩时，给定体积含有更多的空气，因此其密度或重量增加。相反，当允许空气膨胀时，给定体积含有更少的空气，因此其密度或重量减小。
热量是所有物质的一种属性。从早期的科学研究中，我们了解到热量是分子的运动。因此，热量被定义为分子运动的总能量。我们还了解到，密度大的空气比密度小的空气含有更多的分子。两者可能具有相同的平均运动，因此具有相同的温度，但密度更大的空气中分子更多，总能量以及因此的热量程度更大。我们无法直接测量热量，但我们可以用温度计测量温度。

media.article:
Domestic airline passengers will be able to make phone calls and send e-mails from the sky from next year, a local carrier has said.
Shenzhen Airlines said on Monday it has signed an agreement with Geneva-based On-Air to provide passengers with in-flight communications services on all its planes by the end of 2009. The service will be introduced on three aircraft ahead of the 2008 Olympics, on two routes——from Shenzhen to Beijing and Shenzhen to Shanghai, the airline said.
Passengers will be able to use their own electronic devices to send e-mails and short messages, make calls and surf the Internet, it said. The service is expected to be of particular interest to business travelers.
According to a survey at the end of last year, China had 4.55 million mobile-phone subscribers. Ninety-three percent of those polled said they wished they could send and receive e-mails and messages while on a plane.
Despite the introduction of the new service, passengers will still be prevented from using their cell phones and laptops when the plane is taking off and landing, or flying at less than 3,000m above sea level, the official said.
The official said the new service would not lead to an increase in ticket prices, but users of Chinese mobile-phone services will be charged the international roaming rate for any calls made from the sky.
Shenzhen Airlines is the first Chinese carrier to provide such in-flight communications services. Its president, Li Kun, said installing the facilities will cost 4.5 million yuan ($596,000) per aircraft. However, the project still needs to be approved by the Civil Aviation Administration of China, a source said.

translation.stem: 哪家航空公司是首家提供机上通信服务的中国航空公司？

translation.article:
一家国内航空公司表示，从明年开始，国内航空乘客将能够在空中拨打电话和发送电子邮件。
深圳航空周一表示，已与总部位于日内瓦的 On-Air 签署协议，到 2009 年底在其所有飞机上为乘客提供机上通信服务。该航空公司表示，该服务将在 2008 年奥运会之前在三架飞机上引入，涉及两条航线——深圳至北京和深圳至上海。
乘客将能够使用自己的电子设备发送电子邮件和短信、拨打电话和上网。预计该服务对商务旅客尤其有吸引力。
根据去年年底的一项调查，中国拥有 455 万手机用户。93% 的受访者表示，他们希望在飞机上能够收发电子邮件和信息。
该官员表示，尽管引入了这项新服务，但在飞机起飞和降落或飞行高度低于海平面 3000 米时，乘客仍将被禁止使用手机和笔记本电脑。
该官员表示，新服务不会导致机票价格上涨，但中国移动电话服务的用户从空中拨打的任何电话将按国际漫游费率收费。
深圳航空是首家提供此类机上通信服务的中国航空公司。其总裁李昆表示，安装这些设施每架飞机将花费 450 万元人民币。然而，有消息称，该项目仍需获得中国民用航空局的批准。

media.article:
Aircraft control systems are classified as primary and secondary. The primary control systems consist of those which are necessary for the safe flight of an aircraft, such as ailerons, elevator and rudder. Secondary control systems improve the performance feature of the aircraft or reduce the control forces by the pilots. Examples of secondary control systems are wing flaps and trim systems.
The ailerons are located on the outboard trailing edges of both wings and are used to rotate the aircraft around the longitudinal or roll axis. These surfaces move in opposite directions from each other and are controlled by rotation of the control wheel. When the control wheel is rotated to the left, the right aileron moves down and the left aileron moves up. This causes the airplane to roll to the left. When the control wheel is turned to the right, and opposite aileron movement results and the airplane rolls to the right.
The pitch motion around the lateral or pitch axis is controlled by elevator moved by fore and aft movement of the control wheel. A change in position of the elevators modifies the camber of the airfoil which increases or decreases lift. When the pilot wants to raise the aircraft nose, back pressure is applied on the control wheel. This moves the elevator up, resulting in a negative angle of attack. This causes the tail to move down and the nose of the aircraft to move up. The rudder moves the aircraft about the vertical, or yaw axis resulting in a motion known as yaw. The movement of the rudder is to the left or to the right and is controlled by the movement of the rudder pedal in the cockpit. Pressure applied to the right rudder pedal displaces the rudder to the right. This increases the camber on the left side of the vertical tail surface, creating a low pressure area on the left and a high pressure area on the right side. The resulting pressures cause the tail to move left and the nose of the aircraft to move right. Applying pressure to the left rudder pedal provides an opposite reaction. The rudder is used in conjunction with the ailerons to coordinate turns.

translation.stem: 以下哪组词不能归为一类？
options: A. 纵轴、滚转、副翼、转弯
B. 俯仰、横轴、升降舵、下降、转弯
C. 方向舵、偏航、垂直轴、转弯
D. 滚转、副翼、转弯、垂直轴

translation.article:
飞机操纵系统分为主操纵系统和辅助操纵系统。主操纵系统包括对飞机安全飞行所必需的那些系统，例如副翼、升降舵和方向舵。辅助操纵系统改善飞机的性能特性或减小飞行员的操纵力。辅助操纵系统的例子包括襟翼和配平系统。
副翼位于两侧机翼的外侧后缘，用于使飞机绕纵轴或滚转轴旋转。这些操纵面彼此反向运动，通过操纵盘的旋转来控制。当操纵盘向左旋转时，右副翼向下移动，左副翼向上移动。这导致飞机向左滚转。当操纵盘向右旋转时，产生相反的副翼运动，飞机向右滚转。
绕横轴或俯仰轴的俯仰运动由升降舵控制，通过操纵盘的前后移动来驱动。升降舵位置的变化改变了翼型的弯度，从而增加或减少升力。当飞行员想要抬起飞机机头时，向操纵盘施加向后压力。这将升降舵向上移动，产生负迎角。这导致机尾向下移动，飞机机头向上移动。
方向舵使飞机绕垂直轴或偏航轴运动，产生称为偏航的运动。方向舵向左或向右移动，由驾驶舱内方向舵脚蹬的移动来控制。向右方向舵脚蹬施加压力使方向舵向右偏转。这增加了垂直尾翼表面左侧的弯度，在左侧产生低压区，在右侧产生高压区。由此产生的压力导致机尾向左移动，飞机机头向右移动。向左方向舵脚蹬施加压力则产生相反的反应。方向舵与副翼配合使用以协调转弯。

media.article:
Certain laws of nature are utilized in flight. The first requirement is the appropriate flying shape, or airfoil, which is commonly called the wing. This is shaped like a flattened teardrop. The rounded rear edge is called the trailing edge. The aircraft is supported by the earth’s atmosphere. What we think of as air is really a mixture of gases that have mass and weight but no shape.
The forces that act on the airplane in flight are weight, lift, thrust and drag. Weight refers to the force of gravity that acts on the plane and everything in it at anytime. In order to fly, the plane must create a force stronger than the forces of its own weight. This is called lift. The lift is produced by the movement of the air around the wing as the plane moves through it. When the wing moves through the air quickly, a low pressure area vacuum is created on top of the wing, and a high pressure area is created under the wing. It is like a cushion of air on which the plane rests and stays during flight. An airplane propelled by the thrust of its jet engines can be thought of as flying on this cushion of air. Drag is the total resistance of the air to the aircraft passing through it. It may occur when the wing moves through the air to create lift, or it may occur when the landing gear is lowered.
The pilot has controls that enable him to operate the plane as it moves through the air. He can cause it to ascend, descend, or remain level. He can also make turns in the air. This is called banking. The controls utilize the forces of the air by making slight adjustments or changes in the wings or the tails. When the flaps-the hinged sections of the wing are raised or lowered by the pilot, they can cause the aircraft to increase lift, decrease landing speed, and so forth. Similarly, changes in the tail of the aircraft cause the plane to turn to the right or to the left.
Weather and atmospheric factors also affect flight. A headwind, or wind blowing against the nose of the plane, will decrease speed. A tailwind will have the opposite effect. Altitude also affects flight. There is a huge river of wind that circles the globe generally from west to east at anywhere from 2500-50000ft above the surface of the earth. This is called the jet stream. As much as two hundred miles an hour may be added to the speed of a plane flying west to east within the jet stream.

translation.stem: 当我们放下或收起襟翼时，飞机将______。
options: B. 增加升力并降低着陆速度

subs.stem: The airplane flying west to east within jet stream will ______ ．

translation.stem: 在急流内自西向东飞行的飞机将______。

translation.article:
飞行中利用了某些自然规律。首要条件是适当的飞行形状，即翼型，通常称为机翼。其形状类似于扁平的水滴。圆形的后缘称为机翼后缘。飞机由地球大气支撑。我们所认为的空气实际上是一种具有质量和重量但没有形状的气体混合物。
作用于飞行中飞机上的力是重力、升力、推力和阻力。重力是指始终作用在飞机及其内部一切物体上的地球引力。为了飞行，飞机必须产生一个强于其自身重力的力。这被称为升力。升力是由飞机穿过空气时空气绕机翼运动产生的。当机翼快速穿过空气时，在机翼上方产生一个低压区真空，在机翼下方产生一个高压区。这就像一层气垫，飞机在飞行中依靠它支撑和保持。由喷气发动机推力推进的飞机可以被认为是在这个气垫上飞行。阻力是空气对穿过它的飞机的总阻力。它可能在机翼穿过空气以产生升力时发生，也可能在起落架放下时发生。
飞行员拥有操纵装置，使其能够在飞机穿过空气时操控飞机。他可以使飞机上升、下降或保持水平。他还可以在空中转弯。这被称为坡度转弯。操纵装置通过对机翼或机尾进行微调或改变来利用空气的力量。当机翼的铰接部分——襟翼——由飞行员放下或收起时，它们可以使飞机增加升力、降低着陆速度等。类似地，飞机机尾的改变使飞机向右或向左转弯。
天气和大气因素也会影响飞行。顶风，或吹向机头的风，将降低速度。顺风则产生相反的效果。高度也会影响飞行。有一条巨大的风带环绕地球，通常从西到东，位于地球表面上方 2500 到 50000 英尺的任何高度。这被称为急流。在西向东飞行于急流中的飞机速度可以增加多达两百英里每小时。

media.article:
Before the airplane begins to move, thrust must be exerted. It continues to move and gain speed until thrust and drag are equal. In order to maintain a constant airspeed, thrust and drag must remain equal, just as lift and weight must be equal to maintain a constant altitude. If in level, the engine power is reduced, the thrust is lessened, and the airplane slows down. As long as the thrust is less than the drag, the airplane continues to decelerate until its airspeed is insufficient to support it in the air.
Likewise, if the engine power is increased, thrust becomes greater than drag and the airspeed increases. As long as the thrust continues to be greater than the drag, the airplane continues to accelerate. When drag equals thrust, the airplane flies at a constant airspeed.
Straight-and-level flight may be sustained at speeds from very slow to very fast. The pilot must coordinate angle of attack and thrust in all speed regimes if the airplane is to be held in level flight. Roughly, these regimes can be grouped in three categories: low-speed flight, cruising-flight, and high-speed flight.

subs.stem: In order to maintain a constant airspeed, ______.
options: A.  thrust must be greater than drag
B.  thrust must be less than drag
C.  thrust must be equal to drag
D.  lift must be equal to weight
answer: C.  thrust must be equal to drag

translation.stem: 为了保持恒定的空速，______。
options: A. 推力必须大于阻力
B. 推力必须小于阻力
C. 推力必须等于阻力
D. 升力必须等于重力

subs.stem: If the air is to accelerate, ______.
options: B.  the aircraft must maintain a constant airspeed
C.  the aircraft must maintain a nose down attitude

answer: D.  trust must be greater than drag

options: B. 飞机必须保持恒定空速
C. 飞机必须保持机头向下姿态
D. 推力必须大于阻力

options: B.  lift is less than weight

answer: B.  lift is less than weight

subs.stem: If the airplane is to be held in level flight,  ______.
options: A.  the pilot must coordinate angle of attack and thrust
B.  it should maintain a constant speed
C.  it should increase its angle of attack
answer: A.  the pilot must coordinate angle of attack and thrust

translation.stem: 如果要使飞机保持水平飞行，______。
options: A. 飞行员必须协调迎角和推力
B. 它应保持恒定速度

translation.article:
在飞机开始移动之前，必须施加推力。飞机继续移动并加速，直到推力和阻力相等。为了保持恒定的空速，推力和阻力必须保持相等，正如为了保持恒定高度，升力和重力必须相等一样。如果在水平飞行中，发动机功率减小，推力减小，飞机减速。只要推力小于阻力，飞机就会继续减速，直到其空速不足以在空中支撑它。
同样，如果发动机功率增加，推力变得大于阻力，空速增加。只要推力继续大于阻力，飞机就会继续加速。当阻力等于推力时，飞机以恒定空速飞行。
平直飞行可以从非常慢到非常快的速度进行。如果要使飞机保持水平飞行，飞行员必须在所有速度范围内协调迎角和推力。粗略地说，这些速度范围可以分为三类：低速飞行、巡航飞行和高速飞行。

media.article:
Unlike landplane operations at airports, seaplane operations are often conducted on water areas at which other activities are permitted. Therefore, the seaplane pilot is constantly confronted with floating, objects, some of which are almost submerged and difficult to see - swimmers, skiers, and a variety of watercraft. Before beginning the takeoff, it is advisable to taxi along the intended takeoff path to check for the presence of any hazardous objects or obstructions. Thorough scrutiny should be given to the area to assure not only that it is clear, but that it will remain clear throughout the takeoff. Operators of motorboats and sailboats often do not realize the hazard resulting from moving their vessels into the takeoff path of a seaplane.
To accelerate during takeoff in a landplane, propeller thrust must overcome only the surface friction of the wheels and the increasing aerodynamic drag. During a seaplane takeoff, however, hydrodynamic or water drag becomes the major part of the forces resisting acceleration. This resistance reaches its peak at a speed of about 27 knots, and just before the floats or hull are placed into a planning attitude.
Several factors greatly increase the water drag or resistance: heavy loading of the aircraft, or glassy water conditions in which no air bubbles slide under the floats or hull, as they do during a choppy water condition. In extreme cases, the drag may exceed the available thrust and prevent the seaplane from becoming airborne. This is particularly true when operating in areas with high density altitudes (high elevations/high temperatures) where the engine cannot develop full rated power. For this reason the pilot should also practice takeoffs using only partial power to simulate the long takeoff run usually needed when operating at water areas where the density altitude is high and/or the seaplane is heavily loaded.

D.  observe the swimmers along the path
answer: B.  make sure it is clear along the intended takeoff path

options: D. 危险的物体或障碍物

subs.stem: During a seaplane takeoff, ______ is the major part of the forces resisting acceleration.

translation.stem: 在水上飞机起飞过程中，______是抵抗加速的主要力量。

subs.stem: The water drag is influenced greatly by ______ ．

translation.stem: 水阻力受______影响很大。

subs.stem: A heavily loaded seaplane takes off under high density altitude requires ______ takeoff run.

translation.stem: 重载的水上飞机在高密度高度下起飞需要______起飞滑跑距离。

translation.article:
与在机场的陆上飞机运行不同，水上飞机的运行通常在水域进行，而这些水域也允许进行其他活动。因此，水上飞机飞行员经常面临漂浮物，其中一些几乎没入水中且难以看到——游泳者、滑水者和各种船只。在开始起飞前，建议沿着预定的起飞路径滑行，以检查是否存在任何危险的物体或障碍物。应彻底观察该区域，不仅要确保其通畅，而且确保在整个起飞过程中保持通畅。摩托艇和帆船的驾驶者通常没有意识到将他们的船只驶入水上飞机起飞路径所产生的危险。
为了在陆上飞机起飞时加速，螺旋桨推力必须克服机轮的地面摩擦力和不断增加的气动阻力。然而，在水上飞机起飞过程中，水动力阻力成为抵抗加速的主要力量。这种阻力在大约 27 节的速度时达到峰值，正好在浮筒或机身进入滑行姿态之前。
有几个因素会大大增加水阻力：飞机负载过重，或镜面水面条件（在这种条件下，没有气泡像在波浪水面条件下那样滑入浮筒或机身下方）。在极端情况下，阻力可能超过可用推力，阻止水上飞机离地。在密度高度较高（高海拔/高温）的区域运行时尤其如此，因为发动机无法发挥全部额定功率。因此，飞行员还应该练习仅使用部分功率起飞，以模拟通常在密度高度较高和/或水上飞机负载较重的水域运行时所需的长起飞滑跑。

media.article:
Obstacle clearance is based on the aircraft climbing at 200ft per nautical mile, crossing the end of the runway at 35ft AGL, and climbing to 400ft above the airport elevation before turning unless otherwise specified in the procedure. This is the basic obstacle clearance specification. A slope of 152ft per mile, starting no higher than 35ft above the departure end of the runway, is assessed for obstacles. A minimum of 48ft of obstacle clearance is provided for each mile of flight. If no obstacles penetrate the 152ft per mile slope, IFR departure procedures are not published. So far so good. If nothing is specified on the SID or in the takeoff section of the airport plan chart, then all you need to do is meet the above climb criteria. If obstacles penetrate the slope, obstacle avoidance procedures are specified. These procedures may be a ceiling and visibility to allow the obstacles to be seen and avoided; a climb gradient greater than 200ft per mile, detailed flight maneuvers, or a combination of the above. In extreme cases, IFR takeoff may not be authorized for some runways. Unless you have some really bad problems, any aircraft capable of IFR flight is able to meet the standard obstacle climb gradient.
Climb gradients are specified when required for obstacle clearance. Crossing restrictions in the SIDs may be established for traffic separation or obstacle clearance. When no gradient is specified, the pilot is expected to climb at least 200ft per mile to MEA unless required to level of fly a crossing restriction. Perhaps we should get our heads together here. We are used to thinking in terms of climbing and descending at a certain feet per minute rate. These climb gradients are based on climbing so many feet per mile. To meet these restrictions, the rate of climb will be a function of both airspeed and ground speed. The faster you fly across the ground, the higher your rate of climb will have to be to meet the climb gradient. Climb gradients may be specified to an altitude/fix, above which the normal gradient applies. Some procedures require a climb in visual conditions to cross the airport at or above an altitude. The specified ceiling and visibility minimums will be enough to allow the pilot to see and avoid obstacles near the airport. Obstacle avoidance is not guaranteed if the pilot maneuvers farther from the airport than the visibility minimum. This is a very important point. If you are given an IFR clearance with a two-mile visibility restriction, it is your responsibility to stay within two miles of the airport until above the ceiling specified in the same clearance. You must remain in visual conditions to see and avoid any obstacles. That segment of the procedure which requires the pilot to see and avoid obstacles ends when the aircraft crosses the specified point at the required altitude. Thereafter, standard obstacle protection is provided.

subs.stem: The basic obstacle clearance specifies that aircraft turns when ______ ．
options: A.  climbing to 400 ft above the airport elevation
B.  crossing the end of the runway at 35 ft AGL
C.  climbing at 200 ft per nautical mile
answer: A.  climbing to 400 ft above the airport elevation

translation.stem: 基本障碍物间隔规定飞机在______时转弯。

subs.stem: If obstacles penetrate ______ , obstacle avoidance procedures are specified.

translation.stem: 如果障碍物穿透______，则规定障碍物规避程序。
options: A. 每海里 200 英尺坡度
B. 每海里 150 英尺坡度

subs.stem: To meet a certain climb gradient in a tailwind condition, your rate of climb should be ______ ．

translation.stem: 在顺风条件下为满足特定爬升梯度，你的爬升率应______。

subs.stem: To guarantee standard obstacle protection, the aircraft ______ ．
options: A.  must be out of specified visibility minimum
B.  must be within specified ceiling
C.  must be within the specified visibility minimum
D.  mustn't be below the specified ceiling
answer: C.  must be within the specified visibility minimum

translation.stem: 为保证标准障碍物保护，飞机______。
options: A. 必须超出规定的能见度最低标准
B. 必须在规定的云底高范围内
C. 必须在规定的能见度最低标准范围内
D. 不能低于规定的云底高

translation.article:
障碍物间隔基于飞机以每海里 200 英尺的速率爬升，以高于地面 35 英尺的高度通过跑道末端，并爬升到高于机场标高 400 英尺后才能转弯，除非程序中另有规定。这是基本的障碍物间隔规范。评估障碍物时，使用从跑道离地端上方不低于 35 英尺开始的每英里 152 英尺的坡度。每飞行一英里提供至少 48 英尺的障碍物间隔。如果没有障碍物穿透每英里 152 英尺的坡度，则不发布 IFR 离场程序。到目前为止一切顺利。如果在 SID 或机场平面图的起飞部分中没有规定任何内容，那么你需要做的就是满足上述爬升标准。如果有障碍物穿透坡度，则规定障碍物规避程序。这些程序可以是：允许看到并避开障碍物的云底高和能见度；大于每海里 200 英尺的爬升梯度；详细的飞行机动；或以上组合。在极端情况下，某些跑道可能不允许 IFR 起飞。除非你遇到一些非常糟糕的问题，否则任何能够进行 IFR 飞行的飞机都能够满足标准的障碍物爬升梯度。
当为障碍物间隔需要时，会规定爬升梯度。SID 中的穿越限制可能是为交通间隔或障碍物间隔而制定的。当未规定梯度时，飞行员应至少以每海里 200 英尺的速率爬升到 MEA，除非需要改平或满足穿越限制。也许我们应该在这里达成共识。我们习惯于以每分钟一定英尺数的速率来考虑爬升和下降。这些爬升梯度基于每英里爬升多少英尺。为了满足这些限制，爬升率将是空速和地速的函数。你在地面上飞得越快，你的爬升率就必须越高才能满足爬升梯度。爬升梯度可以规定到一个高度/定位点，在此之上则适用正常梯度。某些程序要求在目视条件下爬升，以在或高于某一高度飞越机场。规定的云底高和能见度最低标准将足以让飞行员看到并避开机场附近的障碍物。如果飞行员机动飞行到远离机场的距离超过能见度最低标准，则不保证障碍物规避。这是非常重要的一点。如果你获得了带有两英里能见度限制的 IFR 许可，你有责任保持在机场两英里范围内，直到高于同一许可中规定的云底高。你必须保持在目视条件下以看到并避开任何障碍物。程序中要求飞行员看到并避开障碍物的那一段，在飞机以要求的高度飞越指定点后结束。此后，提供标准的障碍物保护。

media.article:
As air rises and expands in the atmosphere, the temperature decreases. There is an atmospheric anomaly that can occur; however, that changes this typical pattern of atmospheric behavior. When the temperature of the air rises with altitude, a temperature inversion exists. Inversion layers are commonly shallow layers of smooth, stable air close to the ground. The temperature of the air increases with altitude to a certain point, which is the top of the inversion. The air at the top of the layer acts as a lid, keeping weather and pollutants trapped below. If the relative humidity of the air is high, it can contribute to the formation of clouds, fog, haze, or smoke, resulting in diminished visibility in the inversion layer.
Surface-based temperature inversions occur on clear, cool nights when the air close to the ground is cooled by the lowering temperature of the ground. The air within a few hundred feet of the surface becomes cooler than the air above it.

subs.stem: An air rises and expands in the atmosphere, the temperature normally ______ .

translation.stem: 空气在大气中上升和膨胀时，温度通常______。

subs.stem: What are the characteristics of inversion layers?
options: A.  Commonly thick layers of bumpy, stable air close to the ground
B.  Commonly deep layers of smooth, stable air well above the ground
C.  Commonly shallow layers of smooth, stable air close to the ground
D.  Commonly shallow layers of smooth, unstable air close to the ground
answer: C.  Commonly shallow layers of smooth, stable air close to the ground

translation.stem: 逆温层的特征是什么？
options: A. 靠近地面的厚而不平坦的稳定空气层

C. 靠近地面的浅层平滑稳定空气层
D. 靠近地面的浅层平滑不稳定空气层

subs.stem: The air layer acts as a ______ , keeping weather and pollutants trapped.

subs.stem: What conditions can contribute to the formation of clouds, fog, haze, or smoke, resulting in diminished visibility in the inversion layer?

answer: B. The high relative humidity of the air

translation.stem: 什么条件可能有助于云、雾、霾或烟的形成，导致逆温层内能见度降低？

subs.stem: Surface-based temperature inversions occur on ______.
options: A.  clear, cool nights when the air close to the ground is cooled by the lowering temperature of the ground
B.  clear, hot nights when the air close to the ground is cooled by the lowering temperature of the ground
C.  clear, cool day when the air close to the ground is cooled by the lowering temperature of the ground
D.  clear, cool nights when the air close to the ground is cooled by the increasing temperature of the ground
answer: A.  clear, cool nights when the air close to the ground is cooled by the lowering temperature of the ground

translation.stem: 基于地面的逆温发生在______。
options: A. 晴朗凉爽的夜晚，此时靠近地面的空气因地面降温而冷却
B. 晴朗炎热的夜晚，此时靠近地面的空气因地面降温而冷却
C. 晴朗凉爽的白天，此时靠近地面的空气因地面降温而冷却
D. 晴朗凉爽的夜晚，此时靠近地面的空气因地面升温而冷却

translation.article:
当空气在大气中上升和膨胀时，温度降低。然而，可能会发生一种大气异常现象，改变这种典型的大气行为模式。当空气温度随高度升高时，存在逆温层。逆温层通常是靠近地面的平滑、稳定空气的浅层。空气温度随高度升高到某一点，即逆温层的顶部。该层顶部的空气像一个盖子，将天气和污染物困在下方。如果空气的相对湿度较高，可能有助于云、雾、霾或烟的形成，导致逆温层内能见度降低。
基于地面的逆温发生在晴朗、凉爽的夜晚，此时靠近地面的空气因地面温度降低而冷却。地表以上几百英尺内的空气变得比其上方的空气更冷。

media.article:
A pilot begins each international flight (or any flight) by getting all the pertinent facts and applying them correctly. In some countries computers play a large part in this preparation. Of a possible dozen routes of flight, a computer can automatically take into account the aircraft’s capabilities against the weather, types of clouds, winds aloft, altitudes, and distance to the final destination, to produce a flight plan or several possible courses from which the aircraft commander can choose. Without a computer, all the variables of flight must be taken into account and figured by hand. No matter which method is used, the pilot should scrupulously review the details in order to decide the best route of flight. He or she will then go to the weather forecaster for a briefing.
The forecaster needs to know the type of aircraft the pilot is flying, the estimated time of departure (ETD), the proposed route and flight altitude, cruising airspeed, estimated time of arrival (ETA) and any other information which will assist in visualizing this particular flight. When the briefing is over, the pilot should have received the following flight information: weather for takeoff and climb, forecast weather en-route, forecast weather for destination and alternates.
The pilot now applies this important information to the proposed flight in order to judge what to expect at the point of takeoff, en-route, and at the destination or alternates. As a rule, if the departure is delayed longer than one and one half hours, the weather is re-checked. In addition to checking the weather, a pilot also checks the NOTAMs. Other members of the crew also play a vital role in assembling the hundreds of facts and details needed for the flight. The copilot figures the weight and balance of the aircraft by computing the position of either the passengers and their baggage or the cargo the plane is carrying. Computations are also made for fuel consumption, flight time endurance, and other details needed in preparation and filing of a flight plan. Out on the flight lines - hours before the arrival of the flight crew - other people have been deeply involved in the many, many details involved in launching a modern jet aircraft.
The plane is fuelled. A crew chief or team of mechanics check the plane from the pressure in its tires to the oil in its engines. The purpose of the flight plan is to relay the desires of the pilot to the air traffic controller. IFR flight plans are normally filed in base operations at least thirty minutes prior to proposed takeoff, so that they can be transmitted to the appropriate air traffic service. Flight plans can also be filed by radio if no other means are available-except for those involving buffer zones or an ADIZ (air defense identification zone).

translation.stem: 以下哪项不正确？
options: A. IFR 飞行计划通常在计划起飞前至少 30 分钟提交
B. 天气预报对飞行计划非常重要
C. 计算机可以完成所有手工完成的工作
D. 并非所有飞行计划都可以通过无线电提交

translation.article:
飞行员开始每次国际飞行（或任何飞行）时，都要获取所有相关事实并正确应用它们。在一些国家，计算机在此准备工作中扮演着重要角色。在可能的十几条飞行航线中，计算机可以自动考虑飞机的能力与天气、云的类型、高空风、高度以及到最终目的地的距离，生成一个飞行计划或几条可能的路线供机长选择。如果没有计算机，必须手工考虑和计算所有飞行变量。无论使用哪种方法，飞行员都应严格审查细节，以决定最佳飞行航线。然后他将去找天气预报员进行简报。
预报员需要知道飞行员所飞行的飞机类型、预计起飞时间、提议的航线和飞行高度、巡航空速、预计到达时间以及任何有助于设想此次特定飞行的其他信息。简报结束时，飞行员应收到以下飞行信息：起飞和爬升天气、航路预报天气、目的地和备降场预报天气。
飞行员现在将这些重要信息应用于提议的飞行，以判断在起飞点、航路上以及目的地或备降场预期会遇到的情况。通常，如果离场延迟超过一个半小时，则重新检查天气。除了检查天气外，飞行员还会检查 NOTAM。
其他机组成员在汇总飞行所需的数百个事实和细节方面也发挥着至关重要的作用。副驾驶通过计算乘客及其行李或飞机所载货物的位置来计算飞机的重量和平衡。还会计算燃油消耗、飞行续航时间以及准备和提交飞行计划所需的其他细节。在飞行前数小时，在外场——其他人员早已深入参与到启动现代喷气式飞机所涉及的众多细节中。
飞机加油。一名机组长或一组机械师检查飞机，从轮胎气压到发动机滑油。飞行计划的目的是将飞行员的意图传达给空中交通管制员。IFR 飞行计划通常在计划起飞前至少三十分钟在基地运行部门提交，以便能够传输到相应的空中交通服务机构。如果没有其他方式，也可以通过无线电提交飞行计划——涉及缓冲区域或防空识别区的除外。

media.article:
Wind shear is a sudden, drastic shift in wind speed and/or direction that may occur at any altitude in a vertical or horizontal plane. It can subject your aircraft to sudden updrafts, downdrafts, or extreme horizontal wind components, causing loss of lift or violent changes in vertical speeds or altitude. In fact, when landing during gusty conditions, you should use a power-on approach and landing to minimize the effect of the wind and to retain positive control of the aircraft. Wind shear is associated with temperature inversions, the jet stream, thunderstorms, and frontal inversions. With a temperature inversion, it occurs when cold, calm air is near the surface and warmer air aloft is in motion. This can be a hazard during climbouts or approaches when your airspeed is relatively slow. Be alert for a sudden change in airspeed and carry an extra margin of speed if you suspect an inversion.
With fronts, the most critical period is either just before or just after frontal passage. With a cold front, wind shear occurs just after the front passes and for a short time afterward. Studies indicate the amount of wind shear in a warm front is generally much greater than in a cold front. The wind shear below 5,000 ft AGL in a warm front may last for approximately six hours. The most critical period is before the front passes; the problem ceases to exist after it passes.
A microburst is an intense, localized downdraft of brief duration which spreads out in all directions when it reaches the surface. This creates severe horizontal and vertical wind shears which pose serious hazards to aircraft, particularly those near the surface. An individual microburst typically covers an area of less than two and a half miles in diameter at the surface and usually lasts no longer than 15 minutes. Peak winds last two to four minutes and attendant downdrafts can be as strong as 6,000 ft per minute.
Any convective cloud can produce this phenomenon. Although microbursts are commonly associated with heavy precipitation in thunderstorms, they often occur in virga. If there is no precipitation, your only cue may be a ring of dust at the surface. If you suspect the presence of microbursts in your local area, delay your takeoff or landing.

subs.stem: Which of the following statements is true?
options: A.  When a cold front just passes, delay your flight for a period of time is beneficial
B.  If you suspect a microburst in your local area, don't fly or land
C.  During gusty conditions, you should use power-on approaches and landing, otherwise you can't control your plane
D.  Wind shear is hazardous only to departing and landing aircraft
answer: A.  When a cold front just passes, delay your flight for a period of time is beneficial

translation.stem: 以下哪项陈述是正确的？
options: A. 冷锋刚过时，延迟飞行一段时间是有益的
B. 如果你怀疑当地有微暴流，不要飞行或着陆
C. 在阵风条件下，应使用带功率进近和着陆，否则无法控制飞机
D. 风切变只对离场和着陆飞机有危险

translation.article:
风切变是风速和/或风向的突然剧烈变化，可在任何高度的垂直或水平平面内发生。它可能使你的飞机遭受突然的上升气流、下降气流或极端水平风分量，导致升力损失或垂直速度或高度的剧烈变化。实际上，在阵风条件下着陆时，你应该使用带功率进近和着陆，以最大限度地减少风的影响并保持对飞机的正向控制。风切变与逆温层、急流、雷暴和锋面逆温相关。对于逆温层，当冷而平静的空气靠近地表而上方较暖的空气在运动时，就会发生风切变。这在你爬升或进近期间空速相对较慢时可能是一种危险。要警惕空速的突然变化，如果你怀疑存在逆温层，应保持额外的速度裕度。
对于锋面，最关键的时期是锋面通过之前或之后的一段时间。对于冷锋，风切变发生在锋面通过之后及其后的一小段时间内。研究表明，暖锋中的风切变量通常远大于冷锋。暖锋中低于 5000 英尺 AGL 的风切变可能持续大约六个小时。最关键的时期是锋面通过之前；锋面通过后问题不复存在。
微暴流是一种强烈的、局地的、持续时间短暂的下沉气流，当它到达地面时向各个方向扩散。这会产生严重的水平和垂直风切变，对飞机构成严重危害，尤其是那些接近地面的飞机。单个微暴流在地面通常覆盖直径小于 2.5 英里的区域，通常持续时间不超过 15 分钟。峰值风速持续两到四分钟，伴随的下沉气流可强达每分钟 6000 英尺。
任何对流云都可能产生这种现象。虽然微暴流通常与雷暴中的强降水相关，但它们经常出现在雨幡中。如果没有降水，你唯一的线索可能是地面的一圈尘土。如果你怀疑当地存在微暴流，请延迟你的起飞或着陆。

media.article:
After receiving clearance to takeoff from runway 23L, the captain ordered F/O to perform Before Takeoff Checklist and confirm runway 23L Final Clear, then execute time set and make right turn to line up using tiller and then transfer control to F/O. F/O did not confirm on flap 20 Set from the checklist due to read back of the takeoff clearance to the tower as he performed the Before Takeoff Checklist.
F/O pushed the TOGA switch when he saw the thrust was approaching 1.1 EPR but Auto Trust did not operate, so the captain advanced the thrust by manual. At this moment, the warning horn sounded and F/O called “we’d better Reject Takeoff” but the captain decided to continue takeoff based on his misjudgment of lightweight(633,280lbs) and sufficient runway. The captain pulled down the flap lever and took the control and continued to takeoff. The aircraft lifted off with flap 4.9, flap 10 when passing 35 ft, flap 20 when passing 156 ft, which led the aircraft to takeoff without the required flap setting. Warning horn sounded for 22 seconds until V1, 128 kts during takeoff and the captain realized Less Flap when rotated and increased speed quickly by maintaining less pitch(less than 10 degrees).

subs.stem: What were the flight doing while they were lining up?
options: A.  They were performing Before Takeoff Checklist.
B.  The F/O was controlling the aircraft.
C.  They were verifying runway 23L Final Clear.
D.  They were setting the time.
answer: D.  They were setting the time.

translation.stem: 飞机在对准时他们在做什么？

C. 他们正在确认 23L 跑道最终通畅。
D. 他们正在设定时间。

answer: C.  Because he was reading back takeoff clearance to the ATC.

C. 因为他正在向 ATC 复诵起飞许可。

subs.stem: Why did the F/O call “we’d better Reject Takeoff”?
options: A.  Because Auto Thrust did not operate.
B.  Because the warning horn sounded at the moment of approaching 1.1 EPR.

answer: B.  Because the warning horn sounded at the moment of approaching 1.1 EPR.

options: A. 因为自动推力没有工作。

C. 因为机长手动前推了推力。

subs.stem: Why didn’t the captain abort the takeoff?
options: A.  Because the captain thought the runway is long enough.
B.  Because the captain advanced the thrust manually.
C.  Because the captain thought the warning horn was not that urgent.
answer: A.  Because the captain thought the runway is long enough.

subs.stem: What was the flap setting at the moment of lift-off?

translation.stem: 离地时的襟翼设定是多少？

translation.article:
在收到从 23L 跑道起飞的许可后，机长命令副驾驶执行起飞前检查单并确认 23L 跑道最终通畅，然后执行时间设定并使用手操轮向右转弯对准跑道，然后将操纵移交给副驾驶。副驾驶在执行起飞前检查单时，由于向塔台复诵了起飞许可，未确认检查单上的襟翼 20 设定。
当副驾驶看到推力接近 1.1 EPR 时按下了 TOGA 电门，但自动推力未工作，因此机长手动前推推力。此时，警告喇叭响起，副驾驶呼叫“我们最好中断起飞”，但机长基于对轻重量和足够跑道的误判决定继续起飞。机长拉下襟翼手柄并接管操纵，继续起飞。飞机在襟翼 4.9 时离地，通过 35 英尺时襟翼 10，通过 156 英尺时襟翼 20，这导致飞机在未达到所需襟翼设定的情况下起飞。起飞过程中警告喇叭持续响了 22 秒直到 V1（128 节），机长在抬轮时意识到襟翼不足，并通过保持较小俯仰姿态快速增加速度。

media.article:
Other factors will affect the accuracy of GPS receivers. One of these is that the speed of light varies as it goes through the atmosphere. This might result in a receiver error of up to 12 feet. The clock error alone might add up to 2 feet. Built-in receiver errors might come to another 4 feet. The worst case satellite selection errors might add up to another 25 feet, and the ephemeris error will result in 2 more feet. AU of this adds up to somewhere between 10 and 30 feet of error. One other error has until recently prevented GPS from being used for precision IFR approaches: selective availability (SA).
Two frequencies are used by the satellites. The precision code (P-code) is used by military and the course acquisition code (C/A code) is used by the public. The military can arbitrarily induce an error into the C/A – code, which can impart an error of up to 300 feet without the user being aware of it. Naturally, an error of this magnitude would prevent the pilot from using GPSs for a precision approach.
To overcome this, ground stations are being provided that “know” their position precisely. This ground station will then derive its own GPS position, recognize any error, and then transmit that difference to the user's receiver so that the receiver’s computer will then be able to give a precise location with repeatable accuracy of within 3-10 meters (roughly 10-30 feet.) This is called differential GPS (DGPS). It is this precision that is enabling aircraft to make fully-occupied autopilots landings, and which, someday, will make all other types of navigation obsolete.
ARNAV Systems, Inc. has two types of GPS receivers available. The first type uses a separate receiver (GPS-506) that interfaces with the FMS-5000 or R-5000 receiver. This combination will provide the pilot with either a loran or GPS position, whichever signal is deemed most accurate. The pilot can also manually select either mode of navigation. The three-dimensional accuracy in the GPS mode (without SA) is within 15 meters horizontally and 90 feet vertically. The second GPS available from ARNAV is the pure GPS receiver, the STAR 5000. The only physical difference between this receiver and the FMS-5000 is apparently the name on the lower right hand side, the letters “GPS” on the upper left hand side, and the absence of the “XP” light on the STAR 5000.

options: A. 它将为飞行员提供罗兰或 GPS 位置

C. 精度在水平 15 米和垂直 90 英尺以内

translation.article:
其他因素也会影响 GPS 接收机的精度。其中之一是光速在穿过大气层时发生变化。这可能导致高达 12 英尺的接收机误差。仅时钟误差就可能增加 2 英尺。内置接收机误差可能再增加 4 英尺。最坏情况下的卫星选择误差可能再加 25 英尺，而星历误差将再导致 2 英尺误差。所有这些加起来会产生 10 到 30 英尺的误差。直到最近，另一个误差阻止了 GPS 用于精密 IFR 进近：选择性可用性。
卫星使用两个频率。精密码供军方使用，粗捕获码供公众使用。军方可以任意向 C/A 码引入误差，这可能会在用户未察觉的情况下造成高达 300 英尺的误差。自然，如此量级的误差会阻止飞行员使用 GPS 进行精密进近。
为克服这一问题，正在提供已知自身精确位置的地面站。该地面站将推导出自身的 GPS 位置，识别任何误差，然后将该差值传输到用户的接收机，使得接收机的计算机能够以 3-10 米以内的可重复精度提供精确位置。这被称为差分 GPS。正是这种精度使得飞机能够进行全程自动驾驶仪着陆，并且有朝一日将使所有其他类型的导航过时。
ARNAV Systems 公司提供两种类型的 GPS 接收机。第一种类型使用一个单独的接收机，与 FMS-5000 或 R-5000 接收机接口。这种组合将为飞行员提供罗兰或 GPS 位置，以被认为最精确的信号为准。飞行员也可以手动选择任一导航模式。GPS 模式下的三维精度在水平方向在 15 米以内，垂直方向在 90 英尺以内。ARNAV 提供的第二种 GPS 是纯 GPS 接收机 STAR 5000。该接收机与 FMS-5000 之间唯一的物理区别似乎是右下角的名称、左上角的字母“GPS”以及 STAR 5000 上没有“XP”指示灯。

media.article:
If you are in contact with a Tower, a Center, or an FSS when either a distress or/and urgency develops, the communication would obviously be directed to that facility. A distress message should start with the word “Mayday” and be repeated at least three times. (Mayday comes from the French, “M' aides, “is pronounced “Mayday”, and means “help me.”).In an urgency, the term “Panpan,” again repeated three times, starts the radio transmission.
*Distress messages have absolute priority over all others. And the word “Mayday” commands radio silence on the frequency in use.
*Urgency messages have priority over all others except distress. “Panpan” warns other stations not to interfere with urgency transmissions.
If, as the pilot, you have not been receiving services from an ATC facility, and if time permits, the Center. Tower or FSS in whose area of responsibility you are flying, should be contacted on the appropriate frequency. By so doing, a more rapid response to your call is likely, which is another reason for listing the various en route frequencies in the preflight planning and having the list immediately available in the cockpit.
Conditions might be such, though; that there is no time to search for the correct frequency, even though a list had been prepared. In that event, two other frequencies can be used: the emergency-only frequencies of 121.5 or 243.0. Both have a range - that is generally limited to line of sight. 121.5 is guarded by direction finding stations and certain military and civil aircraft, while 243.0 is guarded by military aircraft. In addition, both frequencies are guarded by military Towers, most civil Towers, FSSs, and radar facilities.
Again, if time permits, the most logical facility to try to reach first is the ARTCC in whose area you are flying, but the ARTCC’s emergency frequency capability does not normally extend to its radar coverage limits. If the ARTCC does not respond when called on 121.5 or 243.0 address the call to the nearest Tower or FSS.
Time is crucial in a distress condition, but as much of the following as possible. Preferably in the order suggested should be communicated:
(1) Mayday, Mayday, Mayday or Panpan, Panpan, Panpan
(2) Name of facility addressed
(3) Aircraft identification and type
(4) Nature of distress or urgency
(5) Weather
(6) Pilot's intentions and request
(7) Present position and heading, or if lost, last known Position, time and heading since that position.
(8) Altitude or flight level
(9) Hours and minutes of fuel remaining
(10) Any other useful information，as visible landmarks, aircraft color, emergency equipment on board, number of people on board.
(11) Activate the ELT (Emergency Locator Transmitter) if installation permits.

translation.stem: 对仅用于紧急情况的频率的呼叫应首先直接发给______。
options: D. 你所在区域的 ARTCC

translation.article:
如果你在与塔台、中心或 FSS 联系时发生遇险和/或紧急情况，通信显然应指向该设施。遇险信息应以“Mayday”一词开头，并至少重复三次。在紧急情况下，术语“Panpan”（同样重复三次）开始无线电发射。
* 遇险信息拥有绝对优先权。“Mayday”一词命令当前使用频率保持无线电静默。
* 紧急信息除遇险外拥有优先权。“Panpan”警告其他电台不要干扰紧急发射。
如果你作为飞行员尚未从 ATC 设施获得服务，并且时间允许，应联系你所飞行区域的相应中心、塔台或 FSS。这样做，你的呼叫更有可能得到更迅速的响应，这也是在飞行前计划中列出各种航路频率并将该列表立即置于驾驶舱中的另一个原因。
然而，情况可能如此：即使已经准备了列表，也没有时间搜索正确的频率。在这种情况下，可以使用另外两个频率：仅用于紧急情况的频率 121.5 或 243.0。两者都有作用范围——通常限于视线范围。121.5 由测向站以及某些军用和民用飞机值守，而 243.0 由军用飞机值守。此外，这两个频率都由军用塔台、大多数民用塔台、FSS 和雷达设施值守。
同样，如果时间允许，最合乎逻辑的首先尝试联系的设施是你飞行区域的 ARTCC，但 ARTCC 的紧急频率能力通常不扩展到其雷达覆盖范围。如果 ARTCC 在 121.5 或 243.0 上未响应，请将呼叫发给最近的塔台或 FSS。
在遇险情况下时间至关重要，但应尽可能多地传达以下信息。最好按建议的顺序传达：
(1) Mayday, Mayday, Mayday 或 Panpan, Panpan, Panpan
(2) 所呼叫的设施名称
(3) 飞机识别标志和型号
(4) 遇险或紧急情况的性质
(5) 天气
(6) 飞行员的意图和请求
(7) 当前位置和航向，如果迷航，则提供最后已知位置、自该位置以来的时间和航向
(8) 高度或飞行高度层
(9) 剩余燃油的小时和分钟数
(10) 任何其他有用信息，如可见地标、飞机颜色、机载应急设备、机上人数
(11) 如果安装允许，激活 ELT

media.article:
As I was about to commence the turn onto the crosswind leg, I spotted the helicopter that was supposedly joining downwind for the right hand helicopter circuit about a mile away in our 11 o’clock, at the same altitude and heading to pass just behind us, i.e., it was right in the middle of the fixed-wing circuit! If I made the turn, I estimated we would have passed within 300m of each other, so I elected to extend upwind and turn crosswind a short while later.
At the time the sun was in my 5 o’clock, and I estimated that I was ‘enjoying’ the forecast visibility of 5-7 KM, however, I knew that I was cutting my own flight short. I’d estimate the visibility “into sun” was probably about 1-2 KM – even from only 1500’ it was barely hard to see any landmarks towards the sun. My suspicion is that the helicopter pilot never saw my aircraft despite stoves, navigation lights and landing lights, and quite possibly they never even know they had passed through an active fixed-wing circuit.
The visibility reported by ATC is measured horizontally in all directions by an n observer on the ground. In conditions of low level haze, the flight visibility, measured forward of an aircraft in flight and the air-ground (slant) visibility may be significantly less than that reported by ATC. Also, the flight visibility into sun, particularly at low sun angles, will often be much reduced. The effect of haze on visibility should be covered in basic flight training.

translation.article:
当我正要在三边开始转弯时，我发现那架据称要加入右旋直升机起落航线 Downwind 的直升机位于大约一英里外我们的 11 点钟方向，在同一高度，航向将从我们后方穿过——也就是说，它正好在固定翼飞机的起落航线中间！如果我转弯，我估计我们之间的最近距离会在 300 米以内，所以我选择延长 Upwind，稍后再转弯进入 Crosswind。
当时太阳在我的 5 点钟方向，我估计我“享受”着预报的 5-7 公里的能见度，但是，我知道我在缩减自己的飞行余度。我估计“朝向太阳”的能见度可能只有 1-2 公里——即使仅仅从 1500 英尺高度，也很难看清朝向太阳方向的任何地标。我的怀疑是，直升机飞行员从未看到我的飞机，尽管有频闪灯、航行灯和着陆灯，而且很可能他们甚至不知道他们穿过了一个活跃的固定翼飞机起落航线。
ATC 报告的能见度是由地面观察员在所有方向上水平测量的。在低空霾的条件下，飞行能见度和空地能见度可能显著低于 ATC 报告的数值。此外，朝向太阳的飞行能见度，尤其是在太阳角度较低时，通常会大大降低。霾对能见度的影响应在基础飞行训练中涵盖。

media.article:
The need for prudence when flying through known areas of forecasted severe weather is well recognized. Presently, ATC procedures call for controllers to assist pilots, particularly when operating on IFR flight plans, in avoiding known areas of severe weather. It is important to remember, however, that there are limitations to an air traffic controller's capability for providing such assistance. The controller’s primary responsibility is to provide safe separation between aircraft. No additional services can be given which distract or derogate from that primary responsibility. Additionally, ATC radar equipment, communications congestion, and traffic may also limit and reduce a controller’s ability to provide any additional service.
To a large degree, the assistance that might be given by air traffic control depends upon the weather information available to controllers through weather reporting channels or by pilots actually requesting to avoid severe weather areas. Because severe weather is extremely transitory in nature, the information available to controllers may be of only limited value, if any – unless frequently updated by pilot reports or radar weather information. In-flight reports from pilots in direct communications with controllers can give vital weather information. Pilots wishing to avoid severe weather along the route should request deviations from route and/or altitude as far in advance as possible. Obtaining clearance to avoid severe weather can often be accommodated in the en-route areas, away from terminal areas, because there is less traffic congestion. In the terminal areas, ATC coordination is complicated by complex departure routes and adjacent airports. Consequently, controllers are less likely to be able to accommodate requests for weather detours. ATC also has other problems in handling the weather. Under certain conditions, echoes received from precipitation render ATC radar unusable because of clutter. To avoid disruption in radar service, modifications have been made too many radars to reduce precipitation clutter.
This feature is known as circular polarization. Terminal radar systems use this feature to reduce clutter during moderate to heavy rain or snow storms in order to keep track of traffic. This necessary process, however, means the controller does not always see the weather on the radar scope. Moreover, if ATC is using a secondary radar system, the controller’s scope displays only those signals emitted by the airborne radar beacon transmitters. With this system the radar scope does not display weather echoes at all. The controllers can, however, be alerted to turn on their normal radar to observe weather, provided this will not result in weather cluttering, rendering the scope unusable for air traffic control. The point to remember is that pilots should realize that the controller’s weather information is often far from complete.
