translation.article:
通常，空气必须达到饱和才能发生凝结或凝华。饱和可能由温度冷却、露点升高或两者共同导致。冷却占主导地位。
在航空界，云被分为 14 类。它们是积云、浓积云、积雨云、碎积云、层云、层积云、雨层云、碎层云、碎雨云、高积云、高层云、卷云、卷积云和卷层云。
云是飞行员的“空中天气路标”。它们向飞行员指示空气运动、稳定性和水汽。云有助于形象地了解天气状况和飞行员在飞行中可能遇到的潜在天气危险。让我们来看看这些“路标”以及如何识别它们。
为识别目的，你只需要关注更基本的云类型，它们分为四个“家族”。家族是：高云、中云、低云。前三个家族根据其形成方式进一步分类。由不稳定空气中垂直气流形成的云是积云，意为堆积或堆叠；其特征是块状、汹涌的外观。由稳定层冷却形成的云是层云，意为成层或分层；其特征是均匀、片状外观。
除上述之外，前缀 nimbo 或后缀 nimbus 意为雨云。因此，有雨降落的层状云是雨层云。产生降水的厚重膨胀的积云是积雨云。破碎成碎片的云通常通过添加前缀 fractus 来识别；例如，破碎的积云是碎积云。
高云家族是卷云状，包括卷云、卷积云和卷层云。它们几乎完全由冰晶组成。这些云底的高度在中纬度地区从 20,000 英尺以上开始。
中云家族包括高层云和高积云。这些云主要是水，其中许多可能是过冷水。这些云底的高度在中纬度地区从约 6,500 到 20,000 英尺。
低云家族包括积云、浓积云、积雨云、碎积云、层云、层积云、雨层云、碎层云、碎雨云。低云几乎完全是水，但有时水可能是过冷水。低于冰点温度的低云也可能含有雪和冰粒子。这些云的云底在中纬度地区从接近地表到约 6,500 英尺。
垂直发展的云包括浓积云和积雨云。这些云通常在冻结层以上含有过冷水。但当积云发展到很高时，云上部的冰冻结成冰晶，形成积雨云。积状云云底的高度从 1000 英尺或更低到 10,000 英尺以上。

media.article:
Most bird strikes on aerodromes involve a small range of species: primarily gulls (especially black-headed and common gulls), waders (mostly lap wing, but also golden plover and oystercatcher), pigeons, corvids, starling, skylark, swift, swallow and martins. There is a clear relation between bird mass and risk that the aircraft will be damaged: small birds (less than 100g) cause damage on less than 3% of occasions when they are struck; medium-sized birds (101g-1000g) -12%; and large birds (over 1000g) - nearly 23%. There is also a strong relation between bird numbers and the chances that the aircraft will be damaged: single birds cause damage on 8% of occasions; small flocks (2-10) - 14%; and larger flocks (11-100 birds) - 40%. Thus, species which are larger than 100g or occur on aerodromes in flocks are most likely to cause damage to aircraft. Gulls, waders, pigeons, corvids and starling all occur commonly on aerodromes, are responsible for the majority of bird strikes and are most likely to cause damage. However, they also respond well to habitat management and active dispersal techniques and are, therefore, controllable.
These birds can be classed as the Priority Group for control. Many other species, most of which are less susceptible to available control measures, are involved in aerodrome bird strikes. Total numbers of bird strikes are not very useful in assessing an aerodrome's bird hazard or performance in controlling it. However, a more detailed breakdown can provide useful insights, as follows:
• Priority group species. If a high proportion of an aerodrome's bird strikes involve priority group species, then the hazard level is probably high and control standard low.
• Multiple strikes. If there are multiple strikes with priority group species, it is an indication that flocks are permitted to build up and remain on or close to runways. Bird strikes, especially multiple strikes, with these species commonly cause damage and, even if the reporting standard is low, these incidents cannot be suppressed. Thus, the hazard is obviously high and there are problems with controlling it.
• Small birds. All aerodromes have many small birds which are inevitably involved in strikes: Skylarks all year but especially in autumn; finches in winter; and swifts, swallows and martins in summer. If the common small birds are absent from an aerodrome's strike statistics, it can be assumed that the true situation is not reflected: the reporting standard must be suspect.
• Less common medium-sized and large species. The significance of the occurrence of these species in an aerodrome's strike records can only be properly interpreted with a detailed knowledge of local conditions.
By adopting priorities of minimizing strikes with: (1) the greatest potential for serious accidents (multiple strikes with large birds); (2) priority group species; and (3) controllable species, and by using the prevalence of strikes with small birds as a check of reporting standard, the data can provide indicators of hazard level and performance which are independent of comparison with other aerodromes.

translation.stem: 以下哪项陈述是正确的？
options: A. 如果常见的小型鸟没有出现在机场的鸟击统计数据中，可以假设报告标准一定是正确的。
B. 如果报告标准低，鸟击事件可以被压制。
C. 鸥、涉禽、鸽子、鸦科和椋鸟都常见于机场。
D. 并非所有机场都有许多不可避免地涉及鸟击的小型鸟。

translation.article:
机场的大多数鸟击涉及少数几种鸟类：主要是海鸥、涉禽、鸽子、鸦科鸟类、椋鸟、云雀、雨燕、燕子和毛脚燕。鸟类的质量和飞机受损风险之间存在明显关系：小型鸟在撞击时造成损害的几率不到 3%；中型鸟为 12%；大型鸟为近 23%。鸟类数量和飞机受损几率之间也存在强相关：单只鸟在 8% 的情况下造成损害；小群为 14%；大群为 40%。因此，体重大于 100 克或在机场成群发生的鸟类最有可能对飞机造成损害。海鸥、涉禽、鸽子、鸦科鸟类和椋鸟都在机场常见，是大多数鸟击的责任方，并且最有可能造成损害。然而，它们对栖息地管理和主动驱散技术反应良好，因此是可控制的。
这些鸟类可被归类为优先控制组。许多其他物种，其中大多数不太容易受到现有控制措施的影响，涉及机场鸟击。
鸟击总数在评估机场鸟类危害或控制性能方面不太有用。然而，更详细的分解可以提供有用的见解，如下所示：
• 优先组物种。如果机场鸟击中优先组物种占很高比例，那么危害水平可能很高，控制标准很低。
• 多次撞击。如果发生优先组物种的多次撞击，表明允许鸟群聚集并停留在跑道上或附近。这些物种的鸟击，尤其是多次撞击，通常会造成损害，即使报告标准较低，也无法压制这些事件。因此，危害显然很高，并且控制存在问题。
• 小型鸟。所有机场都有许多小型鸟，它们不可避免地会涉及撞击：云雀全年，但尤其是秋季；雀类在冬季；以及雨燕、燕子和毛脚燕在夏季。如果常见的小型鸟从机场的鸟击统计数据中缺失，可以假设没有反映真实情况：报告标准必须受到质疑。
• 不太常见的中型和大型物种。这些物种出现在机场鸟击记录中的意义，只有详细了解当地条件才能正确解释。
通过采用优先减少以下鸟击的策略：(1) 严重事故可能性最大的鸟击；(2) 优先组物种；以及 (3) 可控物种，并通过使用小型鸟鸟击的普遍性作为报告标准的检查，这些数据可以提供危害水平和性能的指标，这些指标独立于与其他机场的比较。

media.article:
The decision to provide a stopway and/or a clearwayas an alternative to an increased length of runway will depend on the physical characteristics of the area beyond the runway end, and on the operating performance requirements of the prospective aeroplanes. The runway, stopway and clearway lengths to be provided are determined by the aeroplane takeoff performance, but a check should also be made of the landing distance required by the aeroplanes using the runway to ensure that adequate runway length is provided for landing. The length of a clearway, however, cannot exceed half the length of take-off run available.
The aeroplane performance operating limitations require a length which is enough to ensure that the aeroplane can, after starting a take-off, either be brought safely to a stop or complete the take-off safely. Let’s suppose the runway, stopway and clearway lengths provided at the aerodrome are only just adequate for the aeroplane requiring the longest take-off and accelerate-stop distances, taking into account its take-off mass, runway characteristics and ambient atmospheric conditions. Under these circumstances there is, for each take-off, a speed, called the decision speed; below this speed, the take-off must be abandoned if an engine fails, while above it the take-off must be completed. A very long take-off run and take-off distance would be required to complete a take-off when an engine fails before the decision speed is reached, because of the insufficient speed and the reduced power available. There would be no difficulty in stopping in the remaining accelerate-stop distance available if action is taken immediately. In these circumstances, the correct course of action would be to abandon the take-off.
On the other hand, if an engine fails after the decision speed is reached, the aeroplane will have sufficient speed and power available to complete the take-off safely in the remaining take-off distance available. However, because of the high speed, there would be difficulty in stopping the aeroplane in the remaining accelerate-stop distance available.
The decision speed is not a fixed speed for any aeroplane, but can be selected by the pilot within limits to suit the accelerate-stop and take-off distance available, aeroplane take-off mass, runway characteristics, and ambient atmospheric conditions at the aerodrome. Normally, a higher decision speed is selected as the accelerate-stop distance available increases.
A variety of combinations of accelerate-stop distances required and take-off distances required can be obtained to accommodate a particular aeroplane, taking into account the aeroplane take-off mass, runway characteristics, and ambient atmospheric conditions. Each combination requires its particular length of take-off run.

translation.stem: ______决策速度，如果发动机失效必须放弃起飞。

translation.article:
决定提供停止道和/或净空道作为增加跑道长度的替代方案，取决于跑道末端以外区域的物理特性，以及预期飞机的运行性能要求。要提供的跑道、停止道和净空道长度由飞机起飞性能决定，但也应检查使用该跑道的飞机所需的着陆距离，以确保提供足够的跑道长度用于着陆。然而，净空道的长度不能超过可用起飞滑跑长度的一半。
飞机性能运行限制要求有足够的长度，以确保飞机在开始起飞后，要么能够安全停止，要么能够安全完成起飞。假设机场提供的跑道、停止道和净空道长度仅刚好满足需要最长起飞和加速停止距离的飞机的要求，同时考虑其起飞质量、跑道特性和环境大气条件。在这种情况下，每次起飞都有一个称为决断速度的速度；低于此速度，如果发动机失效必须放弃起飞，而高于此速度，则必须完成起飞。如果在达到决断速度之前发动机失效，完成起飞需要非常长的起飞滑跑和起飞距离，因为速度不足且可用功率减小。如果立即采取行动，在剩余的可用加速停止距离内停止将不会有困难。在这种情况下，正确的做法是放弃起飞。
另一方面，如果在达到决断速度后发动机失效，飞机将有足够的速度和功率在剩余的可用起飞距离内安全完成起飞。然而，由于速度较高，在剩余的可用加速停止距离内停止飞机会有困难。
决断速度对于任何飞机都不是固定速度，而是可以由飞行员在限制范围内选择，以适合可用的加速停止和起飞距离、飞机起飞质量、跑道特性和机场的环境大气条件。通常，随着可用加速停止距离的增加，会选择更高的决断速度。
对于特定飞机，可以获得所需加速停止距离和所需起飞距离的各种组合，同时考虑飞机起飞质量、跑道特性和环境大气条件。每种组合都需要其特定的起飞滑跑长度。

media.article:
Wake vortices are a natural by-product of lift generated by aircraft. An aircraft exposed to the wake vortex circulation of another aircraft can experience an aerodynamic upset which it may or may not be able to easily correct with its control authority, especially when the aircraft is adjacent to the ground. For this reason, numerous Air Traffic Control (ATC) separation standards include consideration of wake vortex behaviour, defining the separation at which operations can be conducted without a concern for a wake vortex hazard. These separation standards have served us well. There has never been a fatal accident in the U.S. due to wake vortex when instrument flight rules (IFR) separations are being provided.
Wake vortex behaviour is strongly dependent on ambient weather conditions. In certain conditions, such as calm winds without turbulence, they linger and last longer. Separation standards and ATC procedures have been designed for the worst conditions with respect to wake behaviour. For this very reason, however, it has long been believed that there may be room for enhancing ATC procedures if wake vortex behaviour were known more precisely.
Over the years, there have been several efforts in the U.S. and abroad to develop technologies that provide improved knowledge of wake behaviour based on environmental conditions, and to implement ATC procedures utilizing this improved knowledge. Some of these efforts are beginning to produce successful results.
The U.S. has deployed a procedure called Simultaneous Offset Instrument Approaches (SOIA). Depending upon the runway geometry, the SOIA procedure can require specific wake vortex related features. SOIA is in the implementation phase at San Francisco (SFO and St. Louis (STL). Several other procedures have been considered over time and some are incorporated in the FAA/NASA Wake Turbulence Research Management Plan (RMP). The RMP has been developed jointly by the FAA and NASA to direct current and future efforts. 
Numerous wake vortex research and implementation efforts are underway in Europe. The German ATC provider Deutsche Flugsicherung (DFS) has developed the High Approach and Landing System (HALS), which has been tested at Frankfurt, Germany, since June 2001. DFS is also developing a Wake Vortex Warning System (WVWS), which appears to have a good outlook for implementation. The current research and development efforts in the U.S. and Europe are being coordinated through the FAA/Eurocontrol Cooperative R&D Action Plan 14.

translation.stem: 以下哪项是本文的最佳标题？
options: D. 尾流研究管理计划

translation.article:
尾流涡旋是飞机产生升力的自然副产品。暴露在另一架飞机尾流涡旋环流中的飞机可能会经历气动扰动，它可能能够也可能无法轻易地通过其操纵权限来纠正，尤其是当飞机接近地面时。出于这个原因，许多 ATC 间隔标准包括对尾流涡旋行为的考虑，定义了可以在不担心尾流涡旋危险的情况下运行的安全间隔。这些间隔标准一直很有效。在提供 IFR 间隔的情况下，美国从未发生过因尾流涡旋导致的致命事故。
尾流涡旋行为强烈依赖于环境天气条件。在某些条件下，例如无湍流的静风，它们会持续更长时间。间隔标准和 ATC 程序是针对尾流行为的最差条件设计的。然而，正是由于这个原因，长期以来人们一直认为，如果能更精确地了解尾流涡旋行为，可能还有改进 ATC 程序的空间。
多年来，美国和国外进行了多项努力，以开发基于环境条件改进尾流行为认知的技术，并利用这种改进的认知实施 ATC 程序。其中一些努力开始产生成功的成果。
美国已部署了一种称为同时偏置仪表进近的程序。根据跑道几何形状，SOIA 程序可能要求特定的尾流涡旋相关功能。SOIA 正在旧金山和圣路易斯实施。随着时间的推移，已经考虑了几种其他程序，其中一些已纳入 FAA/NASA 尾流湍流研究管理计划。该 RMP 由 FAA 和 NASA 联合制定，以指导当前和未来的工作。
欧洲正在进行大量的尾流涡旋研究和实施工作。德国空中交通服务提供商 DFS 开发了高进近和着陆系统，自 2001 年 6 月起在德国法兰克福进行了测试。DFS 还在开发尾流涡旋警告系统，其实施前景似乎很好。美国和欧洲目前的研究和开发工作正在通过 FAA/Eurocontrol 合作研发行动计划 14 进行协调。

media.article:
In the United States the highest number of operations (2,770,303) was recorded at Atlanta (ATL), which had 11 runway incursions during the period. The second highest number of operations (2,703,603) was recorded at Chicago O’Hare Airport (ORD), which had 14 runway incursions during the period. The third highest number of operations (2,662,815) was at Dallas-Fort Worth Airport (DFW), which had 15 runway incursion during the period.
The rates of runway incursions per 100,000 operations at these airports during the period were 0.41 at ATL, 0.52 at ORD, 0.56 at DFW, and 1.28 at LAX.
The geometry of an airport – that is, how the runways and taxiways are configured – has a substantial effect on an airport’s exposure to runway incursions, said David Kurner, regional runway safety program manager for FAA’s Western Pacific Region. 
“At LAX, because of the parallel runways in the southern complex, where aircraft come off the outboard runway (Runway 25L) and cross the inboard runway (Runway 25R) is a problem area,” Kurner said. “That is the area of most concern to us right now.”
The southern-complex runways are the longest runways at LAX. Runway 25L is 11,096 feet (3,384 meters) long and is used primarily for landings. Runway 25R, which is 12,091 feet (3,688 meters) long, is used primarily for departures. 
Of the 30 runway incursions at LAX in 1998 through 2000, five were classified by FAA as Category A incursions, and seven were classified as Category B incursions. Category A and Category B represent major runway incursions where there was a high risk of collision.
Despite efforts to reduce runway incursions at LAX, the problem has persisted, said Thomas Winfrey, spokesman for Los Angeles World Airports, which owns and operates LAX and three other airports in southern California. “We have spent money in many ways,” Winfrey said. “We have installed signage on the airfield – upright stop signs to warn pilots where they are, flashing red lights to help gain pilots’ attention, wider paint striping on hold lines, and extensive training programs with people who move planes around the field. Nevertheless, runway incursions are a continuing problem for us.”
In March 1999, airport-operations managers, airport control tower managers, FAA representatives, airline representatives, pilots, controllers and others met to discuss the runway incursion problem at LAX. A steering committee was created, and six different methods were developed by the committee. In August 2000, LAWA, FAA and United Airlines contracted with NASA to conduct at FFC a study of the six methods. The study did simulations of all six proposals and judged two of them to offer the greatest safety. One was to require all aircraft landing on 25L to turn left off the runway, taxi on a parallel taxiway past the far end of both runways, and then to turn right and cross the extended runways. This resulted in slightly longer taxiing but no actual crossing of Runway 25R. The second procedure was to use two local controllers for the south-complex runways – one local controller for Runway 25L and one local controller for Runway 25R. Details of the study are available at the Flight Safety Foundation. See Data for details and a direct link.

translation.article:
在美国，亚特兰大记录的最高运行架次为 2,770,303 架次，期间发生了 11 起跑道侵入事件。运行架次第二高的是芝加哥奥黑尔机场，为 2,703,603 架次，期间发生了 14 起跑道侵入事件。运行架次第三高的是达拉斯-沃斯堡机场，为 2,662,815 架次，期间发生了 15 起跑道侵入事件。
这些机场在此期间每 100,000 架次运行的跑道侵入率分别为：ATL 0.41，ORD 0.52，DFW 0.56，LAX 1.28。
FAA 西太平洋区域跑道安全项目经理 David Kurner 表示，机场的几何布局——即跑道和滑行道的配置方式——对机场面临跑道侵入的风险有重大影响。
“在洛杉矶国际机场，由于南部综合体的平行跑道，飞机脱离外侧跑道并穿越内侧跑道是一个问题区域，”Kurner 说。“那是我们目前最关注的区域。”
南部综合体的跑道是洛杉矶国际机场最长的跑道。25L 跑道长 11,096 英尺，主要用于着陆。25R 跑道长 12,091 英尺，主要用于离场。
在 1998 年至 2000 年洛杉矶国际机场的 30 起跑道侵入事件中，FAA 将 5 起归类为 A 类侵入事件，7 起归类为 B 类侵入事件。A 类和 B 类代表碰撞风险高的重大跑道侵入事件。
尽管努力减少洛杉矶国际机场的跑道侵入，但问题仍然存在，洛杉矶世界机场的发言人 Thomas Winfrey 说，该公司拥有并运营洛杉矶国际机场和南加州的另外三个机场。“我们以多种方式投入了资金，”Winfrey 说。“我们在机场安装了标牌——直立式停止标志以警告飞行员所在位置、闪烁红灯以吸引飞行员的注意力、等待线上更宽的油漆标线，以及针对在机场移动飞机的人员的广泛培训计划。尽管如此，跑道侵入对我们来说仍然是一个持续存在的问题。”
1999 年 3 月，机场运行经理、机场塔台经理、FAA 代表、航空公司代表、飞行员、管制员和其他人开会讨论了洛杉矶国际机场的跑道侵入问题。成立了一个指导委员会，该委员会制定了六种不同的方法。2000 年 8 月，LAWA、FAA 和美联航与 NASA 签约，在 FFC 进行一项关于这六种方法的研究。该研究对所有六项提案进行了模拟，并判定其中两项提供了最大的安全性。一项是要求所有降落在 25L 上的飞机左转离开跑道，在平行滑行道上滑行经过两条跑道的远端，然后右转穿越延伸的跑道。这导致滑行时间稍长，但无需实际穿越 25R 跑道。第二个程序是为南部综合体跑道使用两名本地管制员——一名负责 25L 跑道，一名负责 25R 跑道。研究详情可在飞行安全基金会获取。

media.article:
I was anticipating a traumatic day due to a planned changeover of support equipment during our shift. The Visual Control Room was very messy with extra and unfamiliar "electronic equipment" all over the limited workspace. 
We carried out the normal quick handover on taking over the aerodrome controller position, but my mind was on the perceived chaos to come later on when the new equipment was made live. I was concerned that the event had been planned to take place during the day, on a potentially busy week day, with no restrictions on the traffic being allowed to operate whilst we ATCOs tried to cope with an untried and unfamiliar system which would instantly replace the old kit. The manufacturer later admitted that they had never done a daytime changeover anywhere before. 
Despite being told there was a jet aircraft backtracking the westerly runway for departure into the circuit, I had not appreciated that my colleague had actually cleared it for take-off prior to my taking over. A light aircraft then reported ready so I looked out at the runway, saw no traffic on it due to the tails of parked aircraft obstructing the view of the runway threshold, missed the contact on the Surface Monitoring Radar due to bright sunlight on the display, and cleared the light aircraft to cross immediately, believing the jet to be still backtracking.  
Fortunately, my colleague was still in the VCR and shouted a warning to me. I was able to stop the light aircraft just past the stop bar on the taxiway, and confirm that the jet was clear for take off when he immediately queried it, having commenced his take-off roll. A near runway incursion with potentially disastrous consequences for which I am still not very proud and still recovering from the shock of what might have happened due to my inattention.

subs.stem: What distracted the attention of the controller before the incident occurred?

B. The sunlight on the Surface Monitoring Radar was too bright.
C. The controller was concerned about a personal problem.

answer: D. The controller was concerned about how an equipment changeover during the day would affect his work.

translation.article:
我预感到将是艰难的一天，因为我们班次期间计划进行支援设备的切换。视觉管制室非常凌乱，有限的工作空间里到处都是额外且不熟悉的“电子设备”。
我们在接管机场管制员职位时进行了正常的快速交接，但我的心思都在稍后新设备启用时即将到来的混乱上。我担心的是，该事件被计划在白天进行，在一个可能繁忙的工作日，并且在我们 ATCO 试图应对一个未经试用且不熟悉、将立即取代旧设备的系统时，没有对允许运行的交通施加任何限制。制造商后来承认，他们以前从未在任何地方进行过白天切换。
尽管被告知有一架喷气式飞机正在西向跑道上反方向滑行准备离场加入起落航线，但我没有意识到我的同事实际上在我接管之前已经许可了它起飞。随后一架轻型飞机报告准备好，所以我看向跑道，由于停放的飞机机尾遮挡了跑道入口的视线，我没有看到跑道上有交通，由于明亮的阳光照射在显示器上，我错过了地面监视雷达上的接触点，于是许可了该轻型飞机立即穿越，以为那架喷气式飞机仍在反方向滑行。
幸运的是，我的同事仍在 VCR 中，向我大声发出了警告。我能够在轻型飞机刚过滑行道停止杆时将其停下，并在喷气式飞机立即提出质疑时确认其已可以起飞，而此时它已开始起飞滑跑。一次险些发生的跑道侵入事件，可能带来灾难性后果，我对此仍不感到自豪，并且仍在从因我疏忽而可能发生的事情的震惊中恢复。

media.article:
At a large Southern European airfield we had been cleared to taxi to the holding point of the departure runway crossing an inner and outer taxiway en route. I released the parking brake and started to move forwards. Almost immediately I saw an A320 of another airline on my left hand side clearly going much faster than we were. My sighting coincided with a call from the ground controller telling us to give way to the opposite direction plane taxiing on Taxiway Mike. The instructions were quite specific, but I had focused my attention completely on the A320 on my left hand side, silently cursing the controller for yet again giving priority to their national carrier. 
As I began to follow my given route to the threshold, the A320 came to stop and I began to wonder what was going to happen next. At that moment, the First Officer said ”Stop!” and I immediately applied the brakes as I sensed the urgency in his voice. Then I saw it, a CRJ moving at speed and coming towards us on the taxiway I was about to cross. I would say without any doubt that, had it not been for the actions of the First Officer, I would have attempted to cross the taxiway, probably causing a collision with the CRJ in the process. 
My fault? I initially tried to tell myself “No”. ATC should have amended our taxi instructions and told us to hold at a suitable apron holding point. They should have been more specific about the aircraft type, shouldn’t they? Basically, of course, it was my fault. I allowed myself to become distracted at a very busy airport at a very busy time of the night. Thanks to the First Officer, I’ll get to have another go at the airfield in question in a couple of days’ time, but my cup of luck is a little emptier than it was, and I have learned from the experience.

translation.article:
在南欧的一个大型机场，我们被许可滑行到离场跑道的等待点，途中穿越内、外滑行道。我松开了停留刹车，开始向前移动。几乎立刻，我看到左边有一架另一家航空公司的 A320，速度明显比我们快得多。我看到的瞬间，恰逢地面管制员呼叫我们，要求我们给相反方向在 Mike 滑行道上滑行的飞机让路。指令非常具体，但我完全把注意力集中在了左侧的 A320 上，心里默默诅咒管制员又一次优先对待其国家载旗航空公司。
当我开始按照指定路线滑向跑道入口时，A320 停了下来，我开始想接下来会发生什么。就在这时，副驾驶说“停车！”，我听到他声音中的紧迫感，立即踩下刹车。然后我看到了它——一架 CRJ 正以很快的速度向我正要穿越的滑行道驶来。我毫不怀疑地说，如果不是副驾驶的行动，我本会试图穿越滑行道，很可能在此过程中与 CRJ 相撞。
我的错？我最初试图告诉自己“不是”。ATC 本应修改我们的滑行指令，告诉我们在一个合适的机坪等待点等待。他们本应对飞机类型更加明确，不是吗？当然，从根本上说，这是我的错。在夜间非常繁忙的时刻，我在一个非常繁忙的机场让自己分心了。多亏了副驾驶，我几天后还能再次尝试在那个机场运行，但我的运气杯比之前空了一些，我从中吸取了教训。

media.article:
Runway entrance lights, also known as RELs, are red and located where a taxiway intersects a runway. RELs illuminate when an aircraft is landing or taking off on a runway. The red lights indicate it is not safe to enter the runway environment.RELs go off just prior to the aircraft reaching the taxiway intersection. This allows air traffic controllers to use anticipated separation and keep the normal flow of traffic moving on the airport surface.
Pilots must stop at the runway hold line and remain stopped when the RELs are on. You should remain clear of the runway any time the RELs are illuminated.When the air traffic control tower issues a clearance to cross or proceed onto the runway, the entrance lights should not be illuminated or should go out by the time the controller finishes the transmission.
Do not enter a runway without an ATC clearance even if the entrance lights have gone out. Runway status lights only visually verify a controller's clearance. They do not act as a substitute for an ATC clearance.
There may be times while waiting for a controller's clearance that you see the entrance lights turn on and off for several cycles. If an air traffic controller gives a clearance and the RELs are illuminated, do not cross over the red lights. Contact ATC and advise that you are stopped due to red lights. If your aircraft has already crossed the runway hold line when the lights illuminate, you should proceed using your best judgment and contact ATC as soon as possible.

subs.stem: What should the pilots do if the aircraft has crossed the runway hold line when the lights illuminate?

B.  The pilots should proceed as planned.

D.   The pilots should handle the situation at their own discretion and advise ATC.
answer: D.   The pilots should handle the situation at their own discretion and advise ATC.

translation.stem: 如果飞机在灯亮起时已经越过了跑道等待线，飞行员应怎么做？
options: A. 飞行员应尽快脱离跑道。
B. 飞行员应按计划继续。
C. 飞行员应立即停在跑道上。
D. 飞行员应自行处理情况并通知 ATC。

translation.article:
跑道入口灯，也称为 REL，是红色的，位于滑行道与跑道交叉处。当飞机在跑道上着陆或起飞时，REL 会亮起。红灯表示进入跑道环境不安全。REL 在飞机到达滑行道交叉口之前熄灭。这使得空中交通管制员能够利用预期的间隔，并保持机场地面上的正常交通流量。
当 REL 亮起时，飞行员必须在跑道等待线处停车并保持不动。只要 REL 亮起，你就应远离跑道。当 ATC 塔台发出穿越或进入跑道的许可时，入口灯应不亮或在管制员完成通话时熄灭。
在未获得 ATC 许可的情况下，即使入口灯已熄灭，也不得进入跑道。跑道状态灯仅用于目视确认管制员的许可。它们不能替代 ATC 许可。
有时在等待管制员许可时，你可能会看到入口灯亮灭几个循环。如果 ATC 发出了许可但 REL 亮着，不要越过红灯。联系 ATC，告知你因红灯而停车。如果你的飞机在灯亮起时已经越过了跑道等待线，你应运用最佳判断继续前进，并尽快联系 ATC。

media.article:
I was recently on a VFR flight, in the right-hand seat. We were passing within a couple of miles of an Aerodrome Traffic Zone (ATZ., under a flight information service from the approach controller. Visibility was good, and there was not too much traffic on the frequency. 
 The controller requested a position report by asking if we were over the Island. This feature is not named over the half million chart, and in the short time I spent looking for it, and asking for clarification, a potential Airprox developed. This was noted by the pilot flying and avoided in good time by an evasive maneuver. 
  We managed to steer clear of the other aircraft, which was a light twin. We were rather annoyed by the lack of traffic warning, and I was angry that the controller caused confusion by referring to a local feature not recognizable to a transiting pilot. This was a distraction that we did not need, being close to an ATZ. We were squawking 7000 Mode C, so I would have thought our position was known. If not, or if there was more traffic than usual on screen, maybe the controller should have asked for a specific squawk. 
 It seems that controllers should be aware that non-local pilots only have charted features by which to navigate. Local knowledge should not be taken for granted.

subs.stem: What was the role of the author in the story?
options: B.  The captain of a VFR flight
C.  The co-pilot of a VFR flight

D.  Because the pilot did not hear the controller clearly.
answer: B.  Because the Island was not shown on the aeronautical chart.

B.   The Airprox was avoided by means of an instruction from the controller.
C.  The Airprox led to a mid-air collision between two aircraft.

answer: A.  The Airprox was avoided with an evasive maneuver from the captain.

subs.stem: According to the author, how can events like the potential Airprox be avoided in the future?

translation.stem: 根据作者，将来如何避免像潜在危险接近这样的事件？
options: A. 管制员不应再假设机组熟悉所有本地特征。
B. 机组应对本地特征有完全的了解。
C. 飞机应配备更好的仪表。
D. 应减少该区域的交通量。

translation.article:
我最近在一次 VFR 飞行中，坐在右座。我们正在距一个机场交通区几英里内通过，接受进近管制员的飞行信息服务。能见度良好，频率上的交通也不太多。
管制员通过询问我们是否在岛屿上空来请求位置报告。这个特征在五十万分之一的航图上没有命名，在我花时间寻找它并要求澄清的短时间内，出现了一次潜在的 Airprox。这被操纵飞行员注意到，并通过规避机动及时避免。
我们设法避开了另一架飞机，那是一架轻型双发飞机。我们对缺乏交通警告感到相当恼火，我很生气管制员通过提及一个过境飞行员无法识别的本地特征造成了混乱。这是我们不需要的分心，因为我们靠近 ATZ。我们应答机编码为 7000 Mode C，所以我本以为我们的位置是已知的。如果不是，或者如果屏幕上的交通比平时多，也许管制员本应要求一个特定的应答机编码。
似乎管制员应该意识到，非本地飞行员只能依靠航图上的特征进行导航。不应将本地知识视为理所当然。

media.article:
A China Southern Airbus A319-100, flight CZ-8444 from Cheongju (South Korea) to Dalian (China) with 90 people on board, was taxiing for departure in reduced visibility and had been instructed to hold short of runway 24R at taxiway A3 about 730 meters down the runway.
 A Korean Airlines Boeing 737-800, registration HL7786 performing flight KE-1958 from Jeju to Cheongju (South Korea) with 137 people on board, had just touched down on runway 24R when the A319 went past the hold short line. The Boeing crew recognized the danger, pulled their aircraft to the very left edge of the runway managing to avoid the A319 by about 10 meters and rolled out without further incident returning the aircraft onto the runway center line.
 South Korea's Ministry of Transport initiated an investigation into the serious incident stating, that the Boeing 737-800 even went beyond the left edge of the runway in order to avoid a collision. The minimum distance between the aircraft were 10 meters (wing tip of B738 to nose of A319). At the time runway 24L was not available for any operation (including taxi). The A319 had been instructed to hold short of the runway, but did not comply with the instruction. The reasons are being investigated.

answer: A.   a flight taxiing out to the runway

translation.stem: 空客 A319 是如何侵入跑道导致这起严重事件的？

B. 空客 A319 的机组没有遵守管制员的指令。

translation.article:
一架中国南方航空空客 A319-100，CZ-8444 航班，从清州飞往大连，机上载有 90 人，在低能见度下滑行准备离场，并已被指示在 A3 滑行道处等待于 24R 跑道外，该位置距跑道约 730 米。
一架大韩航空波音 737-800，注册号 HL7786，执行 KE-1958 航班从济州飞往清州，机上载有 137 人，刚刚在 24R 跑道上着陆，此时 A319 越过了等待线。波音机组意识到危险，将他们的飞机拉至跑道最左侧边缘，设法以约 10 米的距离避开了 A319，并滑跑完剩余距离，无进一步事件，随后将飞机回到跑道中心线上。
韩国交通部对这起严重事件展开了调查，称波音 737-800 甚至越过了跑道左侧边缘以避免碰撞。飞机之间的最小距离为 10 米。当时 24L 跑道不适用于任何运行。A319 已被指示在跑道外等待，但未遵守指令。原因正在调查中。

media.article:
Although I was not on the affected flight, I witnessed the use of the “Stop immediately, acknowledge” call by ATC for wholly inappropriate reasons. Thankfully, the crew concerned either ignored the instruction or didn’t hear it. 
The Tower made the call as the previous departing traffic reported that they may have hit a bird on takeoff. 
Before switching to flying, I trained as a controller, and remember the guidance for the “Stop” command as being non-existent. I’m sure the seriousness of rejecting a takeoff is not fully understood by many controllers. 
In my company, a co-pilot can only call “Stop” for fire, engine failure, configuration warning, runway blocked or serious control difficulties. A captain can call “stop” for any anything, but above 80 knots, this should be limited to the co-pilot’s list of reasons and major malfunctions only. 
 Perhaps guidance for controllers for the “Stop” R/T should be along the lines of: “For life threatening reasons only, that would not be evident to the operating pilots, e.g. an aircraft going around that poses a risk of collision.”

subs.stem: The controller instructed the preceding departing traffic to ________________.

B.  Because the aircraft experienced engine failure

answer: A.   Because the aircraft encountered a bird strike

answer: C.   Many controllers do not appreciate the seriousness of aborting takeoff.

translation.stem: 根据作者，管制员何时应使用“停止”呼叫？
options: A. 管制员可以随时使用“停止”呼叫。
B. 管制员绝不应使用“停止”呼叫。
C. 管制员只应在安全关键原因下使用“停止”呼叫。
D. 管制员应在发出“停止”呼叫前与飞行员商讨。

translation.article:
虽然我不在受影响的航班上，但我目睹了 ATC 因完全不恰当的原因使用“立即停止，确认”呼叫。值得庆幸的是，相关的机组要么忽略了该指令，要么没有听到。
塔台是在前一架离场交通报告他们可能在起飞时撞鸟后发出这一呼叫的。
在转行飞行之前，我接受过管制员培训，并且记得关于“停止”指令的指导是不存在的。我相信许多管制员并不完全理解中断起飞的严重性。
在我所在的公司，副驾驶只能在发生火情、发动机故障、形态警告、跑道被占用或严重操纵困难时才能呼叫“停止”。机长可以因任何原因呼叫“停止”，但高于 80 节时，应仅限于副驾驶所列的原因和主要故障。
或许对管制员“停止”无线电通话的指导应该大致如下：
“仅用于危及生命的原因，且是运行飞行员不易察觉的情况，例如存在碰撞风险的复飞飞机。”
